
---
date: 2023-12-30
to: Rhys Fisher <rhys@unvanity.com>
subject: Re: I'm sorry 🫶
msg_id: 18cb869c27a3a6dd
---

Hey Rhys,

Sure, let’s do a call sometime in the new year. I’ve been super heads down
on my new role, but maybe mid/late Jan when things settle a bit?

There were a few other reasons I became I bit quiet in growth mentor but
the main one was just I became too busy, but I should catch up with Foti
soon.

Appreciate how you’re approaching this, and hope you had a good
holidays/have a good new years.

Matthew

---
date: 2023-12-29
to: Katherine Burson <kburson@umich.edu>
subject: Hiring junior marketer
msg_id: 18cb7f5a2b4ecd82
---

Hi Katherine,
Hope all is well with you and you're having a good holidays
Complete shot in the dark, but if you have any former students (maybe someone
right out of school if they're absolutely stellar, but ideally more like 2-3
years experience) who maybe remind you of me a bit (or generally can think
critically, are motivated/go above basic requirements/want to work in early
stage tech, and are good digital/production/creative/writing "things"), I'm
hiring a community/marketing manager to work for me and would love to talk to
them - https://www.linkedin.com/jobs/view/3795650156 - we have no real
preference on location FYI.
I guess some context, I switched companies from when we last spoke, as my
project was very much a skunk works/future looking product, which was one of the
first to get cut as belts tightened over the summer. I've been working at a
company that basically puts stocks/ETFs/etc on the blockchain with 1:1 backing
 (and generally a highly compliant approach) so they can be freely traded - all
to say if whoever you think of has experience trading/using crypto
and blockchains, that would be highly relevant.
And thanks for the intro to Eli - very interesting guy - I actually have a group
of friends who run a music event production company in Detroit that I set him up
with, so think  they might have some interesting ways to collaborate. Please do
always feel free to make similar connections like that whenever you think to.
I'll also likely be settling a bit more in Detroit come spring, so will let you
know when and will look forward to getting to catch up then :)
Hope you have a great new year!
Matthew

---
date: 2023-12-29
to: Foti Panagio <foti@growthmentor.com>
subject: Re: Marketing/community manager recs
msg_id: 18cb7d990f2cf20c
---

Looking forward to catching up next week - finally got our job post up, let me
know if you think of anyone and please feel free to share anywhere
- https://www.linkedin.com/jobs/view/3795650156/

---
date: 2023-12-17
to: Jonathan Saffari <jonathansaffari@gmail.com>
subject: Following up
msg_id: 18c76546b0029897
---

Hey man,
Sorry it took me so long to get to this. To recap our conversation, it would be
great if you could spend about a day (checking in at about the halfway point to
realign/prioritize for the second half) doing the following:
1) higher priority - make a list of quick win action items (20 or so ideally) to
improve the UI/UX of our product and website. This should made  with the idea
that I will sort/filter them for what is feasible, and drop them in to  our
Jira-like task management tool for engineering. The easier they are to drop in,
the better, so quite granular is good (feel free to group them as you see
appropriate as well).
2) a few points/an outline of wider areas of improvement. This will be what I
take to mgmt, along with your quick win ideas to show evidence of impact/ROI
already, and try and get them to approve you working on these. I would focus
more around product than around brand initially

Some info on Dinari:
https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mLXtRejoKEX00zP_2Tkn94i7-gQyrMf_TlqSD65dpGI/edit#heading=h.uhpwvs2ijhya
Various
links: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1KwbHtnC2xYqMCIuhr8eGAMsF7YfTbPGCdhWeumvhjhI/edit#gid=197296203
In terms of us as a brand, we probably want to be very similar to Circle, and in
general want to be boring, compliant, secure, etc.We want to be very
conservative in how we market, so think companies like Robinhood, WeBull, eToro,
etc. No direct calls to invest, very transparent UI with lots of information and
embedded context
I've also included our brand guidelines here for reference, although I don't
think you'll really need them (let me know if you can't access them, don't
request access though if you can't)
- https://www.figma.com/file/FJ9Non0LMGkWfZK4ZQC44q/Dinari-Brand?type=design&node-id=1831-19&mode=design
I've also included a link to a gift card here so you can try the platform as
part of this - let me know if you have any issues claiming it -
sbt.dinari.com/redeem/VCWT-6QFU-BTDD-HBC6
Let me know if you have any thoughts, questions, etc!
Matthew

---
date: 2023-12-17
to: Yohann Merran <yohann@angelspartners.com>
subject: Re: Thank you!
msg_id: 18c757e5bae90b41
---

No problem at all :) I actually have a few other intros I’ve been meaning
to make, but have just been swamped.

Let’s definitely catch up soon - I’m doing a lot of BD outreach in my new
role and it’d be great if I could potentially get a list from you of a few
segments of fintech companies in emerging markets - I’d probably also be
interested in doing some personal due diligence around investing for a fair
amount of them as well

How’s Thursday or Friday afternoon/evening for you next week, or sometime
the following?

Best,

Matthew


On Thu, Dec 14, 2023 at 12:51 Yohann Merran <yohann@angelspartners.com>
wrote:

---
date: 2023-12-14
to: Foti Panagio <foti@growthmentor.com>
subject: Marketing/community manager recs
msg_id: 18c673da7bb2ce7d
---

Hey Foti,

Hope alls been well with you!

I was wondering if you might have any recommendations for a
marketing/community manager (full time hire, pretty flexible on location),
as you often has a finger to the pulse of these sort of things. I need to
hire a junior at my new company (dinari.com - so
fintech/finance/crypto/web3 experience a big plus) and am probably here for
somewhat of a long haul so want to try and pull out any stops I can :)

Would love to catch up some time as well when you have some time - just let
me know.

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2023-12-14
to: Rhys Fisher <rhys@unvanity.com>
subject: Re: I'm sorry 🫶
msg_id: 18c6658f73059e87
---

Hey Rhys,

Appreciate the message. In general this is water under the bridge to me.

I don’t go out of my way to talk bad about you (or anyone) to anyone, so
much so that it’s one of the reasons i don’t really engage in the growth
mentor community. I have only mentioned some very high level details of
what happened between us when specifically asked about you by a couple of
people I introduced you to, as my name was on the line and it would be
dishonest not to - it’s been at least 2 years since that’s come up.

However, you got very personal in your attacks, flaunted the money you were
making, etc - which are all just no gos in a work environment due to the
potential liability they can create. I’m sorry if it came across that my
intention was in any way to screw you over - it very much wasn’t.

Wish you the best,

Matthew

date: 2023-12-08
to: Eli Wolnerman <eli@beassqueeze.com>
subject: Re: Catch Up
msg_id: 18c4a322f59f793b
---

Hey Eli,
Sorry for my delay - I had been meaning to intro you to Nick for ages and just
kept falling off my to do list - I've been doing 80+ hour weeks getting stuff
stood up at my new job, and just finally got cleared to hire a junior so should
be able to start getting my head above water a bit more.
I'll be back in Detroit in spring most likely for at least a month to find a
place for me to live (and Movement), so should  be around a fair bit more after
that. I just introed  you to Nick though, who is one of my very good friends,
and is way more connected in Detroit than I am, so I imagine you'll have an
interesting conversation :)
Also another interesting person to meet could
be https://www.linkedin.com/in/rphiliplockwood/ - he's one of the nicest people
I know, and is working on refurbishing a block in Woodbridge starting with the
Danish Brotherhood Hall, with a view that a large part of its use case would be
for events.
Hope you've been doing well, and let me know if there's anything I can help with
or potential connections I could make that might be useful :)
Matthew

---
date: 2023-12-08
to: Nick Inchaustegui <inchausn@gmail.com>, Eli Wolnerman <eli@beassqueeze.com>
subject: Nick/Eli Intro
msg_id: 18c4a2c264586894
---

Hi Nick,
Please meet Eli, who was introduced to me by my favorite professor at UofM. He's
an entrepreneur and owns a building in the city that he rents out for regular
event nights. We met last time I was in Detroit and hit it off, and thought you
two would as well.
Eli, meet Nick - we were housemates for 2 years at UofM and now own a rental
property together in SW. He works at McKinsey and runs an event production
company with a group of friends that puts on techno/house/etc shows.
I'll let you two take it from here,
Matthew

---
date: 2023-12-08
to: randall@oconnordetroit.com
subject: Re: FW: New to market : Historic 2 Unit Multifamily / Live Work near Clark Park
msg_id: 18c4a00f204358a0
---

Hi Randall,

Thanks very much for this.

This is definitely of interest to me at first glance, although I think my
ideal location for my next purchase will be somewhere between Woodbridge
and New Center, as I will be planning to move in there. A duplex would be
ideal, but by spring I will probably be looking with a bit more urgency and
will probably be considering apartments/single family or more broad
location for a duplex if I haven’t found something by then. Definitely like
the mixed use aspect though - if this is around in a month or two and
everything seems good on initial walk through, I could see making a low
ball offer.

If Steve and/or Nick are interested in this, I’d run with them for the time
being :)

Best,

Matthew

for before that seems likely to run over, so want  to leave some padding.
Looking forward to catching up!
Matthew

---
date: 2023-11-28
to: romilpd@hotmail.com
subject: Following up
msg_id: 18c13b79e7954f01
---

Hi Romil,
Was great to meet the other week - sounds like we have quite a lot of
interesting crossovers :) On a few points we discussed:
Some potential connections you mentioned from your side that we'd definitely be
keen to make: * SteelDAO
 * Arkham
 * Indian Equities RWA
 * You had also mentioned people at TreasurySpring and McKinsey
   crypto, but if you think those might be less relevant, we can focus on the
   above and circle back


From my side, some intros and paths that might be interesting: * Mike Taylor: co-founder and ex-COO of Ladder agency, now building a Marketing
   Mix Modeling company, which IMO is likely where user/web analytics is heading
   with cookie death/ad blockers/etc (and a bunch of stuff on AI prompt
   engineering). He writes a lot of content and courses, based in Liverpool
 * James Palmer: runs a company that basically scrapes all the data and manages
   comms sequencing, particularly verticalized around use case of
   influencer/affiliate management with a guy named Vin Clancey (Vincent
   Dignan), who was probably the first notable growth hacker in Europe, but was
   super content/social/flash oriented. James founded a dating company for
   gamers with decent traction, based in London, he was an admin of the "London
   startups" facebook group if you were ever in that
 * Ultan Miller: runs a blockchain advisory/fund/venture studio in Dubai, I
   believe originally connected to me by Josh, we might work with him to help
   him facilitate building advanced trading tools as a third-party developer
 * Davis Ballard: close friend of mine, content and strategy agency/freelancer,
   worked for a lot of big names, recently Macallan, Poolsuite/Vacation, etc. 
   Lives  in LA.
 * ManyLabs: this was the replication research project I worked on you may find
   interesting
 * Please find
   linked a bit of a one-pager on Dinari for your
   reference: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mLXtRejoKEX00zP_2Tkn94i7-gQyrMf_TlqSD65dpGI/edit?usp=sharing
 * Gift Card: find
   linked here a gift card for a small amount of Nvidia dShares -
   sbt.dinari.com/redeem/BS6W-SDCQ-TSX4-9T8R - let me know if you have any
   issues claiming :)


Let me know if any of the above are interesting for an intro or there's 
anything I've forgotten, and let's catch up soon/when our paths cross next!
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2023-11-26
to: Ethan Fassett <ethan@glystn.com>
subject: Catching up
msg_id: 18c0d1ca5c03c499
---

Hey Ethan,

Sorry for cancelling the other week in SF. It turns out I likely had a
pinched nerve that had me at about 50% capacity the past two weeks, so just
getting back on top of things now.

Would sometime next week work for a call to catch up?

Matthew

---
date: 2023-11-26
to: Alex Shevelenko <alex@relayto.com>
subject: Email gating pdfs…
msg_id: 18c0d1a457fe7ec7
---

I have a PDF of a B2B sell sheet/one pager for my company (Dinari.com) I
want to email gate (and maybe another couple we would want to host but
currently just have uploaded on our website).

Would it make sense to use relayto for this?

---
date: 2023-11-26
to: Tom McCubbin <tom.mccubbin@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Inforeach DB
msg_id: 18c0d0e8e1f9d8c2
---

Happy thanksgiving to you both! And look forward to seeing you in LA Daniel.


Happy to chat further on this Tom (or both).

I’m not myself an engineer but have worked on a lot of technical products
and in IT consulting, so I get the basic high level value prop - but not so
much the performance/cost improvement potential, ideal customer type, etc.

Just to clarify, you’re looking to sell the tech? Or are you potentially
looking to raise funding or otherwise bring the product to market
initially? Do you have patents filed?

I do have a few people that might be interesting to talk to/could help with
a sale (or really on either front) - NTT DATA, a few successful serial
entrepreneurs in SV, my current engineering team, a few other resources. Or
happy to provide some feedback/advice as you look to go to market if that’s
more your thinking.

Let me know, or happy to jump on a quick call as well if you’d like.

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2023-11-23
to: Jerome Minney <jeromeminney@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Sorry we didn't get to connect today
msg_id: 18bfd6a04551fffd
---

Ack I'm sorry - today is thanksgiving and I've been completely wrapped up
with family - my fault for not realizing when we were confirming scheduling
- how's same time Tuesday or Thursday next week?

---
date: 2023-11-21
to: muto@doublefeather.com
subject: Interest in connecting?
msg_id: 18bf2c00237d6cb6
---

Let me know if you might want to connect - I put some money in their first
round, sounds like they're making some pretty solid progress recently
- https://docsend.com/view/8m52qvdgypkj43ua - https://versus.africa/
Also we'd love to connect with the contact you mentioned in Luxembourg when
you're back up to speed with things.
Let me know if there are any other interesting connections from my side!
Matthew

---
date: 2023-11-21
to: Kemdi Ebi <kemdi@versus.africa>
subject: Running 5 min late
msg_id: 18bf28082b0b645e
---

Sorry, should be on very shortly, previous call running late

---
date: 2023-11-20
to: Jonathan Saffari <jonathansaffari@gmail.com>
subject: Small project
msg_id: 18beeaa92b1b0ea9
---

Hey man,

Would you be interested in doing a small project for me? Basically spending
a day or so tearing down the UI (some design tips, but really much more
UX/UI/product usability/product streamlining/identifying friction points
and recommending improvements/etc) for the new company I’m working for -
dinari.com - basically giving me a list I can dump into the
engineering/design backlog of product improvements to make.

Let me know what you think :)

Hope all is well!

Matthew

date: 2023-11-17
to: CoinMarketCap <support+id795560@coinmarketcap.zendesk.com>
subject: Re: [CoinMarketCap] Re: Dinari - AMZN.d - Add cryptoasset
msg_id: 18bdc434fc9dd2b5
---

We have 18 tokens and will release another 5 every month.

RWAs are an extremely hot industry topic, and we are likely taking one of
the most compliant and legally robust approaches of anyone in this space,
including 1:1 asset backing, SEC transfer agent status, Big 4 Accounting
firm treasury audits, bankruptcy protections to pass holdings through to
end users, and dividend pass through as. We have investment from 500
startups and Balaji Srinivasan, and we have been founders and executives
previously of companies like Freenome, LegalZoom, Samba TV, Crunchyroll,
and more.

It is not feasible for us to pay what you are asking every time we list a
token.

Please let us know how we can proceed.

Additionally, please note that our supply is minted and burned
corresponding to on chain buying and selling (as we purchase or sell the
underlying asset instantly in the background, always maintaining a 1:1
backing, sort of like USDC). Your submission form does not allow for a
token of this nature.

We are working on API end points for you that can provide a real time feed
of supply. However, this still does not really fit in the way you display
circulating/max supply.




On Thu, Nov 16, 2023 at 23:21 Mul (CoinMarketCap) <
support@coinmarketcap.zendesk.com> wrote:

---

subject: Re: [CoinMarketCap] Re: Please change my account email address
msg_id: 18bdae42eb6bde6a
---

Okay, maybe I misinterpreted - let’s review on the planning call tomorrow.
But to summarize: -static cards (both low and high value) would be good
-can we run a giveaway with variable amount cards as are? Are static share
or dollar amounts better? Cc @Chas Rampenthal -I also think we should get
COIN cards quickly (and the ability to create ones for new assets for
upcoming launches)
-lockup on tokens from referrals/giftcards (or only allowing swaps to other
dShares) could be interesting
 -iterating the gift card/referral feature to support a more affiliate
style referral system could be good for working with influencers,
communities, press I’ve also updated the tweet 😅 - appreciate the heads up
Gabe

(specifically Africa, with an emphasis on government lobbying, enterprise
partnerships, and facilitating foreign investment to support the portfolio)
- https://www.linkedin.com/in/koheimuto/

backing (UN, Shell, etc), and thought you may be interested in connecting with
them. It also sounds like they're talking to the SBC program in South Africa.
Let me know if it might be interesting - he'd be keen to connect.
Matthew

date: 2023-11-06
to: James Palmer <jcpgamerdating@gmail.com>
subject: Catching up

Hey man,

Hope all is well with you!

I’m working at a new company and was curious if you might be able to help
us with referrals/affiliates/influencers/lead targeting lists. Company is
dinari.com - basically Circle/USDC but for equities/stocks/ETFs/etc. Team
and funding are pretty wild and well poised for next raise.

We have a live app on Arbitrum and 13 assets (another 5 in a few weeks),
referral program and sign up bonus on line (although we’d need to tweak how
the referral program works to make it an affiliate program), etc - but we
do also KYC and don’t service users in USA, China, etc.

Want to catch up sometime this week?

Best,

Matthew

and an angel investor who backed the first company I worked at out of

and analytics space that I invested in/advise for a bit, and is in LA this
week at a creator event.

Ethan, meet Davis, who is one of my best friends in LA. He works on the
creative and strategy side of marketing/content/growth, and has quite a lot
of experience on campaigns for many notable brands at various stages of
their business.

mention maybe some path for collaboration on the work front.

Matthew

subject: Fwd: DeFi Airdrops: Now Live for Zerion Advocates.

I imagine you’re aware, but just a heads up I got this pretty sure to be
phishing email that isn’t triggering spam filters etc and isn’t done
terribly

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Zerion Devs <claims@zerionclaim.com>
Date: Sat, Nov 4, 2023 at 14:53
Subject: DeFi Airdrops: Now Live for Zerion Advocates.
To: Thorchain User <matteisn@gmail.com>


<#m_-656778486525209863_>

Greetings Zerion User!

We're ecstatic to the launch and airdrop of our goverance token $ZERION!

Want to know if you qualify? :

   - You are required to have a record of at least 7 transactions made
   through Zerion's platform.
   - For eligibility, you need a minimum of 7 interactions with DeFi
   protocols via Zerion during the specified period.
   - You should have been an active user of Zerion for a duration of more
   than 2 days.
   - You are required to have a portfolio with a minimum of 2 distinct
   tokens as seen on Zerion.

Retrieve Your Special $ZERION Airdrop
<https://ghccedj.r.bh.d.sendibt3.com/tr/cl/Q6sp_n2KOZ4dc67l3uOEPFZGGS-pzn7tbILTmwEXoLkm-95ywsxG7ZjVuF27ALFp3R-SBjaEI_zVOom61PgrvQRGuVWkkLmBHefARGM4aT0JPSIM-qxJxYJvnEvC0pFl3Zas3Fxxm13VMnZpUPzeKZSrWl4_FrCtieU-FGSzVmumM629O39FCaD-te8CaS8DQUFDjXbkAz42_H7AsTyEz-m_9avMy6sgml6PjI334ucRaANhcIcBLag>

Attention all blockchain finance navigators! your support has been
instrumental in our journey through decentralized finance! Keep building
your portfolio with us at Zerion as there's no limit to the innovation we
can achieve together! 🌌
Keep Talking,
The Zerion Team

---
date: 2023-11-01
to: Ethan Fassett <ethan@glystn.com>
subject: Re: In the bay til Nov 15
msg_id: 18b8bb5b3888a486
---

Friday could work - or Sunday. Let me know what's ideal for you!
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2023-11-01
to: Ethan Fassett <ethan@glystn.com>
subject: In the bay til Nov 15
msg_id: 18b89483fe8960ae
---

Hey Ethan,

I’m around SF for a few weeks, would love to catch up if you’re here!

Also, I’ve been having to stand up the community/marketing/growth ops at
the new company im at, Dinari. Anything you’re seeing out there that I
should be using in terms of community management tools? Our community is
pretty small (<2k people) but it’s becoming very time consuming.

Would love to see if we could use Glystn down the line when it make sense
as well but imagine we’re a little early :)

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2023-11-01
to: matt@dinari.com
subject: Use Copernicus space press list for targeting
msg_id: 18b8881f59409fa1
---

Copernic Space Web3 marketplace for space assets and investments


https://copernicspace.com/

---
date: 2023-10-31
to: Jerome Minney <jeromeminney@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Setting up a call
msg_id: 18b86dbe086c6c31
---

10.30 am on Thurs  or 10 am on Fri  works well for me - I'm pretty sure the 
currently  time shift is -7 due to mismatched savings time shifts FYI :)

---
date: 2023-10-31
to: elliott@fusionone.co
subject: Following up
msg_id: 18b83cb1a2315004
---

Hey Elliott,
Was great to catch up last week. Following up on a few points:
-Fusion One and Dinari -  it'd be great to connect our founders if there might
be interest in collaboration on your side. We're pretty interested in exploring
how we can branch out beyond traditional securities and commodities, and carbon
credits definitely piqued some interest.
-Vortexa  - Happy to reach out to the founder here if it might be interesting to
connect with, they do data analytics on shipping/logistics, somewhat similar to
Flexport (probably their closest competitor), I did some consulting work for
them on and off for a couple years.
-NTT DATA UK - my old manager  is the Head of R&D/New Ventures/CTO of the UK arm
of NTT DATA. They do quite a bit with various service wrappers of startups for
their clients (which usually involves a pretty sizable equity-free strategic or
in kind investment), as well as had a major client pushing us to do some deep
research into solutions for emissions analytics/optimization/tax management when
I was there a couple years ago. It would be very easy to forward some info to
him and see if he might be interested.
-Ahsan Sohail  - runs a family industrial process optimization consultancy,
manly focused on energy and chemicals, based in Pakistan and Texas and the
countries you would expect, good friend from uni, I'm catching up with him in
the next week or two so will mention either way to him to get his take.
-Kohei Muto  - a friend from boarding school, runs an advisory firm with backing
from the Japanese govt and is setting up an accelerator with backing from UN,
Shell Foundation, etc, with a focus on Africa. They do quite a bit with
infrastructure, telco, logistics, transport. He's in London pretty frequently.

If any of the above sound interesting (apart from Dinari, as that's easy), it'd
be helpful to have a little blurb I can send. I would probably butcher
describing it pretty nicely :)
Hope all is well, and let me know when you wind up stateside/I'll do the same
when I'm in Europe!
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2023-10-31
to: ross@zest.capital
subject: Catching up
msg_id: 18b83aa2834565a3
---

Hey Ross,
Hope all is well with you!
I've been ramping up in a new job at a company (Dinari) where we issue 1:1
backed RWAs (going a very regulated route: SEC registered transfer agent,
audited by KPMG, etc), currently 13 US stocks and ETFs.
The initial vision was pretty similar to Kontinuous to tokenizing startup
equity/distributed cap table management, but pivoted over time to more of an RWA
infrastructure play.
Any chance you'd be up to hop on a call for 20-30 minutes so I can prick your
brain around the state of the market and just general ideas?
And of course would be great to catch up, and happy to help however I can with
what you're working on or make any useful connections if I can - seems like some
solid progress as of late :)
Let me know if there might be a time that would work for you in the next few
weeks.
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2023-10-30
to: an@askria.ai, nick@curia.co
subject: Nick/Adhrita intro
msg_id: 18b82c83285125a1
---

Hi Adhrita,
Please meet Nick, a good friend of mine from uni who is a serial entrepreneur
and engineer with a strong data background solving some very
interesting/complex/blue sky analytics problems. He's working on a few projects
at the moment, one of which sounded like it might have some interesting
crossover with Askria.
Nick, Adhrita is the founder of Askria, which I put some money in to in an early
round a few years ago, and was very well timed for the AI hype wave. She's been
making some significant strides in the business and product over the past year
or so, which I imagine you might have some interesting ideas/feedback around in
terms of some of the tech.
I imagine you two will have an interesting convo - i'll let y'all take it from
here!
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2023-10-30
to: Chris Jantz-Sell <chris@samba.tv>
subject: Re: Catching up
msg_id: 18b82b7ebbbd70c7
---

The 8th is probably a bit better for me if possible, either mid/late morning or
mid/late afternoon - let me know where/when's idea for you!

---
date: 2023-10-30
to: Jerome Minney <jeromeminney@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Setting up a call
msg_id: 18b82ab5adf2dc97
---

Assuming you're in the UK, those probably don't work for me - I'm in SF at the
moment. I could maybe do the  Friday but would  be super late Thurs  night for
me (which isn't abnormal).
Are you a little more flexible afternoons  next week?

---
date: 2023-10-30
to: ashwin@samba.tv
subject: Catching up in SF
msg_id: 18b82a10e8f28425
---

Hi Ashwin,
Hope all is well, and I'm sorry we never got a chance to catch up in the closing
weeks/months during my time at Samba.
I'm in the bay until about the 18th or so - if you happen to be around, I'd love
to grab a coffee or lunch or similar.
Best,
Matthew

date: 2023-10-27
to: Patrick Sheehan <psheehan@elevationsecurities.com>
subject: Re: Monzo
msg_id: 18b732dda579deb7
---

Hi Pat,

Sorry for my delay on this - I believe I was traveling back from London
when you sent this, and then did 3 weeks in South America while working
remotely, so has been a very hectic period and less networked period of
time :)

I received access and assuming it didn’t expire, will set up an account and
poke around next week.

I also will be in the bay (probably more Palo Alto than SF but can come up
to the city) from Oct 31 until Nov 14 - would love to catch up if that
happens to overlap with you. Would love your take on what I’ve been working
on recently - initially on-chain 1:1 asset backed compliant assets
(currently US stocks and ETFs but likely expanding), but eventually looking
to be the decentralized exchange for RWAs - dinari.com

Hope all is well!

Matthew


On Thu, Sep 28, 2023 at 13:06 Patrick Sheehan <
psheehan@elevationsecurities.com> wrote:

msg_id: 18b67770432b412d
---

Hi Colin,
Please meet Kohei, a friend of mine from boarding school who now runs an
interesting fund/advisory business based out of Japan but focused on tech in
Africa.
Kohei, please meet a good friend and former colleague of mine at Startupbootcamp
who is setting up a blockchain-focused venture studio and fund I'm a partner on.
I think you two have a fair bit of overlap from a couple of angles, and maybe
could be some useful intros/deal flow/etc.
I'll let you two take it from here (or happy to join the call if timing
permits).
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2023-10-25
to: Kohei Muto <muto@doublefeather.com>
subject: Re: Following up from call
msg_id: 18b6773a07ad42ca
---

Cool - introed you to my dad, and booked a call for week after next
I'll touch base with Andy, and Colin I can go ahead and make an intro to.
Certainly would be interested in connecting with Softbank (I used to have a
contact in their London office but I believe he left) and Cindy (I didn't know
her particularly well at GS though).
Let me know if Chris gets back to you, or anyone else you think might be
interesting for me to connect with. Also if you get any pre-seed/seed stage deal
flow, let me know - I'm pretty  small ticket, but have had some pretty decent
success in emerging market investments :)

but is traveling a fair bit over the coming weeks, so you may have to chase a
little to book a call :)
Dad, please meet Kohei, who was a year above me at George School and now runs an
advisory/fund out of Japan focused on emerging market tech. One of their
projects is around car trading, and he was keen to connect.
I'll let you two take it from here.

Matthew

Hi CJS,

Hope all is well with you!

---
date: 2023-10-24
to: Kemdi Ebi <kemdi@versus.africa>
subject: Catching up
msg_id: 18b63cde122c414a
---

Hey Kemdi,

Hope all is well with you!

I believe last I saw, Versus was looking at launching some sort of token
based incentive system? Would love to learn more, and hear how things are
going in general.

I’m also working at a company that seems to have a strong use case and
initial traction in emerging markets, particularly Nigeria. Basically we
offer on-chain, 1:1 backed tokenized equities, allowing access for users in
underserved markets to stocks and equities (starting with US blue chips and
ETFs) and fractional shares to lower entry price point. I’d love to hear
your take/if you might have any market research you’d suggest I look at/etc.

Let me know if there might be some times in the coming weeks that might
work for you, and if there’s anything I can help you with!

Best,

Matthew

date: 2023-10-24
to: Mattia Mrvosevic <mattia@eternacapital.com>, Nassim Olive <nassim@eternacapital.com>
subject: Fwd: Identity/Payment - Breakeven - US - Seed
msg_id: 18b62989cdd2f3cd
---

Hi guys,

Hope all has been well. A portfolio company of mine has been doing quite
well and is closing up a seed round (see below) - let me know if you might
be interested in connecting.

We’re also a ways off (we have to wait on the results of a broker dealer
application with FINRA) but have been starting to talk to potential leads
for our A round at the new company I’m working at, dinari.com - and would
love to jump on a quick call and pick your brain on what’s working with go
to market/growth with DeFi/CeFi, if there might be any Eterna companies
that might be interested in working with us, your thoughts in general.

Let me know if either of the above are of interest :)

Best,

Matthew



---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Andry Lebedev <a@swipelux.com>
Date: Tue, Oct 24, 2023 at 07:40
Subject: Identity/Payment - Breakeven - US - Seed
To: Matthew <matteisn@gmail.com>


Matthew, thank you for offering to pass info about Swipelux (
https://www.swipelux.com/), an identity infrastructure with distribution
via payments.

   - We power payments for 50+ platforms across gaming, trading, and
   betting.
   - Integrated digital identity solution into our payments.
   - 396k ARR, at breakeven and growing on average at 719% MoM in Revenue.

We aim to become the world's largest identity platform through our scalable
approach to integrating directly into existing user flows.

We're currently raising a $1.6M seed round and are looking for strong
partners to join our existing VCs such as Morgan Creek(lead), Untapped,
Outliers Ventures, Token Ventures, and Bergtop Ventures.


Please find our deck here <https://docsend.com/view/rpnrrsryrjceeauf>.
Please let me know if you need any additional information to make the
connection.

Best,

date: 2023-10-23
to: Filip Kollert <f@swipelux.com>, jake@dinari.com, booi@dinari.com
subject: Swipelux x Dinari connection
msg_id: 18b5d4a7f5fe68d5

Hi Filip,
Good to catch up just now, and glad to hear there are a number of paths (on/off
ramp, KYC, listing dShares, other partnerships) that are interesting for us to
explore :)
I've looped in Brandon (CPO) and Jake (CTO) so we can continue the conversation
to see where there might be technical/strategic/commercial alignment. Feel free
to loop anyone in from your side, or send along a calendar link/some times for
later this week or early next that might work for you.
Looking forward to talking again soon,
Matthew

---
date: 2023-10-21
to: Alexander Guy <alexanderwguy19@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Catching up
msg_id: 18b53af1cfef9f0a
---

No worries at all!

Really whenever works for me, although I’m on east coast US time this
coming week, so later in the day your time? Happy to plan around you though.

Nothing to prepare, I’m just curious what’s been working for you for
driving growth, what you see working for more strictly-defi protocols, etc.
I’m effectively a one man marketing shop (with solid design and dev support
or I’d be in trouble ha) for the next couple of months, so want to try and
focus on things that actually work (and has been a while since I’ve been in
a role like that :) ).

Let me know when might work for you - Weds/Thurs/Fri is quite flexible for
me.

Looking forward to catching up!

Matthew




On Fri, Oct 20, 2023 at 05:14 Alexander Guy <alexanderwguy19@gmail.com>
wrote:

---
date: 2023-10-19
to: Filip Kollert <f@swipelux.com>
subject: Re: Catching up
msg_id: 18b48b1535773d25
---

Booked something for Monday.

So that was announced shortly before I arrived, but I think we use them
pretty purely for on/off ramp. Reducing friction in on/off ramp and KYC is
a huge priority for us at the moment though, so I imagine you may have some
thoughts around that.

But basically were Circle for equities (and eventually other assets), 1:1
backed RWAs, and taking a super compliant route.

---

Sure, sounds good - also ill be in the bay from Oct 30-Nov 14 (with some a
couple of days in LA in there I’m still sorting). Would love to catch up!

date: 2023-10-18
to: Filip Kollert <f@swipelux.com>
subject: Catching up
msg_id: 18b40f51107fdfb7
---

Hey Filip,

Hope all is well since we last spoke - all signs seem to point to some
solid progress and growth!

Sorry I dropped off after we last spoke - I ended up having some
significant changes at my last job (in addition to quite a bit of travel
and moving), and then started a new role which has been quite a whirlwind
in onboarding.

First, I’d love to catch up a little bit in a week or so once I’ve had a
chance to polish up the notes I had from last call to see if they might
still be relevant to you, and if I can help get any of them off the ground
(although would have to be quite light touch at the moment).

I’d also love to pick you brain a bit in what I’m working on - you can see
a bit more at dinari.com - think Circle but issuing RWAs (currently 13 US
stocks and ETFs), and very compliance-first. I’d be curious if there might
be some opportunity for partnership (us using your tech and/or you/your
clients/your users having access to our assets), as well as just to hear
about what’s been working for you in terms of growth (and more generally
hear about how things have been going :) ).

Let me know if there might be a time that would work for you in the coming
few weeks (probably afternoon/evening Europe time).

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2023-10-18
to: Alexander Guy <alexanderwguy19@gmail.com>
subject: Catching up
msg_id: 18b406323c98f43f
---

Hey Alex,

Hope all is well with you, the job, and the family!

I’m still probably a quarterly active user of Zerion, but looks like some
impressive strides you’ve been making despite the dynamics of the bull
market :)

I recently joined a post-seed startup as Head of Product & Growth working
on highly compliant, 1:1 backed RWAs (think circle for equities or mirror
but compliant and with a treasury vault) called Dinari. We’re starting to
scale up our marketing efforts, and if you might be willing, I’d love to
pick your brain a little on what you’ve seen working for DeFi and general
project launches (as well as catch up in general :) ).

Of course happy to help with anything I can or make a connection or two if
I can.

Let me know if a time might work in the coming couple of weeks.

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2023-10-17
to: maxwell.simons@nansen.ai
subject: Looping back from Labels interview - can you point me in right direction?
msg_id: 18b3c48e5ae6a03b
---

Hi Max,
We caught up maybe 10 or so months ago around a labeling feature you were
working on for Nansen.
I recently joined a company called Dinari  that is building compliant, 1:1
backed on chain RWAs (currently 13 US stocks and ETFs, but expanding), currently
on Arbitrum. We've got quite a bit of funding from some name brand investors,
are an SEC registered transfer agent, have multiple serial founders as 
cofounders, and I'm leading our growth and product scale up, including leading
our listing efforts on various platforms.
I was wondering if you might be able to point me in the direction of someone
internally who might be able to tell us a bit more about how we might be able to
(or what we might need to do to) enable price feeds, token tracking, etc.
Let me know if you might be able to help!
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2023-10-17
to: muto@doublefeather.com
subject: Following up from call
msg_id: 18b3c1d3191ed8c7
---

Great to catch up! Would love to connect with Chris and reconnect with Victor or
anyone else you think might be worth talking to.
For a brief blurb: Dinari is building 1:1 backed, on-chain Real World Assets
(called dShares), starting with US equities (currently 13 stocks and ETFs and
counting somewhat quickly), but with a vision to broaden the types and
geographies of our asset over time (as regulation allows) and add a number of
peripheral features, including developer tools. We're an SEC registered transfer
agent and our treasury is regularly audited by KPMG, raised $7.5m in seed from
top investors, co-founders were the co-founders of Freenome and Crunchyroll.


Let me know which of the below sounds interesting - I believe Pelin and Andy are
in London (as are the two accelerator program managers from MassChallenge and
Google):

Adrian Pang: previously did web3 innovation consulting for UN and WHO, Head of
Strategy at MoonPay, cofounder of a crypto VC, I believe recently left MoonPay
but not sure what he’s doing next - supposed to catch up with him soon - very
international, based on Lisbon - https://www.linkedin.com/in/adrian-pang/




Colin Allison: friend who is setting up web3 infrastructure/fintech venture
studio and fund in Singapore, has run a number of accelerators including
Facebook Korea, corporate innovation/venture consulting for top companies in
Malaysia - https://www.linkedin.com/in/colinallison1/




Andy Shannon: former Head of Global at Startupbootcamp for 5+ years (my boss for
2), over saw  all programs, franchising, scaling operations, partnerships,
structuring, internal alignment (which was very hard with a 50/50 ownership
structure), etc. Now advises a number of funds and scale ups, based in Hampstead
in London - https://www.linkedin.com/in/atshannon1/




Pelin Sahin: my ex gf but close friend still, lots of experience in MENA (COO
for Startupbootcamp Istanbul, then Director of Startupbootcamp Dubai), very
passionate and trying to orient her career more around tech for good -
https://www.linkedin.com/in/pelingonulsahin/




Dean Eisner: I’ll talk to him on Thursday when I see him, but if he isn’t just
not in a work mindset, he’ll be happy to chat




Village Cap/Citi Ventures: need to reconnect with these two, but I’ll raise what
you’re doing




Also I had mentioned Deep Ecosystem and GALI - Deep is an easy connection, GALI
I’d have to make sure my contact didn’t retire. Both have quite solid research.

-https://community.deep-ecosystems.com/

-https://www.galidata.org/




Also the unfortunately somewhat fluffy report I ran aggregating our application
data at SBC for public use: https://www.startupbootcamp.org/




And don’t share, but the internal one, which happy to chat on how to set up
analytics like this if you ever want: 
https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1JlJyUkpw70sNndVyRh7hd0bazNSWp8n4h3r1l3tXP8k/edit#slide=id.p29




Also, if you ever wanted to run a blockchain oriented program (or even FinTech
including blockchain), a couple of friends of mine who run a fund have set up a
few (I actually helped them quite a bit on the ops and setup side, but they were
quite good at partner development, fundraising, pitching, capital connections).
They are all ex-Blackrock and very straightlaced  (I believe they’ve taken
Pension Fund money, which has also closed off quite a bit of the types of
blockchain investments they can make). https://www.eternacapital.com/

---

to: Drew Graham <drew@radish.credit>
subject: Re: Radish Technology Holding Ltd - Advance Subscription Agreement - Matthew Eisner
msg_id: 18b3a0afb84ec2f8

Hi Drew,

I've sent along the funds now - let me know if you have any issue receiving.
Looking forward to seeing what's next for you
Best,
Matthew

date: 2023-10-12
to: Colin Allison <colin@konnectventures.co>
subject: Fwd: Rand Investor Update
msg_id: 18b240fc9684fa65
---

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Ross Murray-Jones <ross@randgallery.com>
Date: Thu, Oct 12, 2023 at 08:16
Subject: Rand Investor Update
To: <matteisn@gmail.com>


Hello Matt,



I hope this message finds you well and you’re enjoying the pleasant Autumn
weather. Below is our quarterly investor update.  Firstly, I thank those
who signed the investor docs for our strategic growth round.



As we’ve recently observed on Cardano with the JPEG Store, a token will
play a crucial role in our platform’s growth next year. Web3 platforms,
particularly NFT marketplaces like Blur, have proven resilient during this
bear market thanks to their practical and well-structured tokens. With the
BTC halving six months away, we have a unique opportunity to prepare for
our token before the next bull cycle. As history has shown, investors who
deploy capital and receive tokens early can make a significant profit.
Timing is crucial in the crypto space.



Thanks to a $250K investment from the Algorand Foundation, our runway has
been extended. However, to continue the development of our token launch and
ensure its success, we will need to raise an additional $500K at least. The
costs and time associated with this undertaking necessitate further
funding.  Despite the challenging market conditions, we remain confident in
the power, versatility, and future potential of NFTs, which act as a
gateway to Web3. We have been engaged in conversations with various
investors and remain cautiously optimistic, but we must take action now to
avoid missing out on this opportunity!


*Cash Balance, Burn and Runway*

   - *Cash Balance: *$685K
- *Q2 Revenue:* $12K in fees from $420K in gross volume. (the downward
   price action of $ALGO continues to impact our fees when converted to
   dollars despite some of our best-performing months).
   - *Rand Market Share:* July = 74%, Aug = 88%, Sep = 84%
   - *Runway:* 10-11 months


*RandX Update (XRPL)*


RandX offers a standout opportunity for us to WIN BIG in the next bull
market, and we have significant support from both the Algorand and Ripple
Foundation to expand and bridge both communities. There are no funded or
full-time marketplaces on XRPL, despite the blockchain having 4.5M wallets,
a $26B market cap, and averaging $1M in NFT sales per month. When NFT
volumes normalise at 1.5% of market cap (the general trend across Web3), we
anticipate NFT volumes reaching $500M as early as next year (a 40X
increase). XRPL is a Web3 stalwart and ours to win.



We launched randx.xyz
<https://d13Xwf04.na1.hubspotlinksfree.com/Ctc/V+113/d13Xwf04/VWmm1B4fNTDRW1LN1Dj8wgb_YW6L2xF554xN87N5ZDtTg3m2ndW69sMD-6lZ3mjW1sGMVz4rgWx1W5xjh7p8GksLZVDb9NB3-2PlPW10jHxS6RbKDjW3J1dB91PC2h_W4bx5dN15PxCgW3KjFdw5fH9bGW30Qh162_ljYrW69stjx292JLgW2MW9sY4BzH1yVxWtkw7098BGW2BWTM123F1_5W3Vmww97mtYb6W5WT7Z3258RqtW4Mz6BV2mk9LSW69CXsx626qCHW2_1FKD7yK_2xN3Yp7zZVjcFdW4_sYK4811Q8kW68CFHk2Xzcywf9fm-3q04>
(in beta) with two launch partners last week, and it received critical
acclaim. You’ll see from this short overview of our incredible XRPL launch
week
<https://d13Xwf04.na1.hubspotlinksfree.com/Ctc/V+113/d13Xwf04/VWmm1B4fNTDRW1LN1Dj8wgb_YW6L2xF554xN87N5ZDtTz3m2ndW6N1vHY6lZ3nhW4PcqWL31gjShW2M8ZCm4c2p1cW78_0275BwQ3wMbvPFR6cky-VkxDxk4RJ29lW2GNcR_1csvqwW5hTmlc5k9n5nW8vBsDy4pYK7_N4XLB3wqk-xjW4qrqpM5qBYFCW4hPvlN2vV2X5N1ZF10F1tGbLW4lR0fn2_jMW1W2VzmqB1TkbMZF36vj6X6Bt7W2X_LPV32Vt2yW30XS6Q3578LYW813Y4-2Yfd34W4MJv4z5rbxxJW7pyc9T7pHN7nW1DfFrz9k0XfqW9dRQJ98dd6Kxf1zr2Cj04>
that we’ve quickly become the go-to NFT platform for creators alongside
xrp.cafe.
[image: Screenshot 2023-10-12 at 11.35.56]
*Rand Gallery and Rand Drops Update (Algorand)*



Our main competitor, ALGOxNFT, has seen a significant decrease in market
share, dropping from 29% in June to 8% last month as we’ve significantly
increased our dominance on Algorand. Over half a dozen marketplaces remain
active, yet we consistently maintain over 80% market share. Revenues are
down given the downward $ALGO price action, but *our position remains a
huge competitive advantage, looking forward towards the next bull run when
volumes return. *



In August, we launched an NFT rewards pilot in collaboration with The
Algorand Foundation, distributing $30,000 to the community. This was a
resounding success. Transaction volumes across our platforms grew by 127%
compared to July to 1.175M $ALGO, making August Rand's second-highest month
in 2023 and one of the top five months ever.
  General Update

   - We have successfully signed and filed taxes in the UK and the US in
   collaboration with PKF UK and PKF US.
   - As a marketing and sales tool, we have launched a drop-ship
   merchandise. We are planning limited and exclusive drops with artists from
   both Algorand and XRPL.
   - We recently attended Token 2049, collaborating with Algorand on their
   booth, meeting investors, and briefly speaking at the Algorand side event.
   - We participated in the Apex XRPL Dev Summit in Amsterdam, connecting
   us with key ecosystem players, including major wallets, other marketplaces,
   aggregators, and many creators (see image below).

[image: IMG_2740]
<https://d13Xwf04.na1.hubspotlinksfree.com/Ctc/V+113/d13Xwf04/VWmm1B4fNTDRW1LN1Dj8wgb_YW6L2xF554xN87N5ZDtTg3m2ndW69sMD-6lZ3pyN7qMf4bbGT2MW8VpGXp5ZFrdJW13FWh_4pK8ZzW5BYHyW6FzrcMW6wCfyl3hh66JW4B1Rzw4-yz6BW47VLMg7pY9qDW6R19Cg462pyJW4dndkZ4M2h5NW3ZDwLP2dMsqyN52Ydsln42T-N8m6P2hRg2NPN5mSmRRS8HNrW33p6jM2z7f60W5q_-hG6QQwx8V5Th6P5h0lD0W7BXrDW5FmLYhW64xHrZ87-H-YW3GD6Pt5RZvjzW8W-dlJ4vM9hMf7LVDq004>
Asks

   - Please reach out if you want to learn more about our strategic growth
   round or token launch. Now is the time to act.
   - We’d still appreciate warm intros to investors, especially angels.

I always appreciate your thoughts on any of the above. You can reach me via
Twitter/Telegram (@rossmurrayjones), email or Calendly
<https://d13Xwf04.na1.hubspotlinksfree.com/Ctc/V+113/d13Xwf04/VWmm1B4fNTDRW1LN1Dj8wgb_YW6L2xF554xN87N5ZDtTz3m2ndW6N1vHY6lZ3m-W7szpd24LD4R0W5b6Ly-7swRCCW61qXJC7G1vZCW8Cv91L6gJ4Y7N6QjCkg9rSBKW8dzw083hCf3hW8nt0-H12DsMJV5y1gV6r2vy_W5ZRbV22Vlbm4V4NZbt5p9wzDVxQHWj1T67m_W3R0mtY9lrX27W1M4hmC4G4NFpW5yl1J78nczQBW7JcLwc95b2YKW1pp4g_77xT8sW61dgck1rymdlW41kxGW1sd_K_W4DdfVl4vtxHhN5qt2Z3vmW_HN2yTbWxLWRgtW5-zQHr37LW4Wf14c0Lz04>
.

backed tokens, and was curious if I might be able to pick your brain a

Best,

Matthew

date: 2023-09-28
to: Patrick Sheehan <psheehan@elevationsecurities.com>
subject: Re: Monzo
msg_id: 18adc3dfe305b0ab
---

Sorry for the delay - I'm on a hectic travel stint right now, and also starting
a new job, so have been a bit less responsive to email than normal.
Just introed you to Pawel - I believe his contact there is the former CFO, but
he probably also has a few second degree connections to Tom as well
Would love to see the investment portal - I'm planning to be in Palo Alto
October 22-24, although will have quite a tight schedule, but would love to
connect, and also get your take on what the new company I joined is building
(basically compliant 1:1 asset backed tokens and dev tools - dinari.com). Also
will likely be back a week or two in December.
Hope all is well, and would love to catch up soon!
Best,
Matthew_____________________________
Matthew Eisner
Website:https://www.matteisn.com/LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/matteisn/
Twitter:@matteisn Skype: matteisnUK: +44 (0)752 350 6891
US: +1 (404)312-6583

---
date: 2023-09-28
to: Pat Sheehan <psheehan@elevationsecurities.com>, pawel@techtree.dev
subject: Pat/Pawel Intro
msg_id: 18adc3b19fed28d2
---

Hi Pat,
Following up on your email, please meet Pawel, who is a serial entrepreneur,
angel investor, and advisor, and has been a very helpful
mentor/collaborator/referrer of mine for quite some time now. He has a very
strong network with European founders and funds, and knows an ex exec at Monzo
who might be interested for you to connect with.
Pawel, please meet Pat, who is the father of one of my very good friends, and
runs a financial services company that I would botch the description of if I
tried. They do quite a bit with scale up/tech companies secondaries, and were
keen to connect with someone at Monzo who might be interested in selling some of
their shares.
I'm sure there are also quite a few other fronts you might have interesting
discussions on as well. Will let you all take it from here!
Best,
Matthew

date: 2023-09-28
to: Ben Harris <benharris@montagwealth.com>
subject: Withdrawal and statements for sept/Oct
msg_id: 18ada5887e7cd61c
---

Hi Ben,

Hope all is well with you. I wanted to inquire on two fronts:

1) could I initiate a withdrawal of $22,000 from the trust to my Truist
account, ideally with the sells happening before Friday (when I expect
congress will default on their budget)?

2) could you send me the account statement for last month, as well as for
September when it’s ready?

Let me know if there’s anything else you need from me.

Thanks!

Matthew

Matthew

documents that didn't require a witness, but accidentally put .com instead of

unsend that. Let me know how I can fix this.
Thanks,
Matthew

date: 2023-09-22
to: julie@blockworks.co
subject: Dinari Press / Intro to Writer
msg_id: 18abc0c835e8154d
---

Hi Julie,
Hope all is well with you from when we last spoke and that operations at
Blockworks are coming together nicely.
I wanted to reach out as I recently started a new role at Dinari, where we're
basically building 1:1 securities-backed tokens and composable smart contracts,
starting with US equities. We got some decent coverage from CoinDesk and
Bloomberg a few weeks ago for a rather large seed funding round.
I'm standing up our marketing, growth, and product functions, and next week
we're announcing referral and sign up bonus programs. I was wondering if there's
be a  writer on your staff that might be interested in covering the announcement
(happy to offer you an exclusive/first release, as well as an interview with
founder/etc in the future)?
You can find attached our press release for the announcement
here: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Hi9oldrj6jTjyzzgEi5FS5_eVkNzdEl9zdOF4Fd2q10/edit?usp=sharing
Let me know if there might be someone that makes sense to connect with. And let
me know if there's anything I can be of help to you with.
Best,
Matthew

Easy enough for me to create email templates
I think it would be better to put together an overview doc to try and narrow the
scope, then do a call next week to go over that. After that, we can provide a
detailed proposal.
Email sequences, CRM config/planning/training, lead gen and nurture process, and
all the email you mentioned are very doable.
Putting together the templates for outcomes is definitely a delivery component -
we shouldn't be doing that work up front for free - MAYBE if that's a sticking
point/unclear to who we pitch then we might do something like this (and
hopefully in the future we can send some cases from past delivery). Identifying
the outcomes/discussing some common cases is certainly something we could do if
the conversation seems to be stalling when we have our discussion to review the
overview doc, but again I'd def prefer to do that as part of delivery. We COULD
say something like that we will plan to create sequences with 3 emails each for
5 lead types and 5 outcomes

date: 2023-09-20
to: cgood@littlepinkbook.com
subject: Quant work for PINK
msg_id: 18ab30560c73a2c6
---

Hi Cynthia,
So sorry about how long this has taken me - you've happened to catch me at what
has been one of the more hectic times I've had (which I very much had expected
to be the opposite apart from travel during this period of time).
First, I've gone through the one-pager you were putting together and added
comments here - what you have looks very good, I had a few tweaks and some ideas
to add more standard metrics, but generally you did a very good job on these.
Find that linked here (and I've shared edit access with
you): https://docs.google.com/document/d/1lWnG9QLRo7O_5BdhEduuo7wckWW28qLU/edit
The files you sent to me are also very useful, and what will likely become your
"data room" that you'll share with people who show serious interest in
purchasing PINK. Particularly the revenue detail report is what they'll likely
most be after.
For the 2 Revenue by Top Clients charts, these are good - you'll likely want to
only use one, and you can drop the % of total revenue column from the table and
just show the dollar figure. Less (but very specific pieces) is generally more
with these sorts of things.
It seemed like this was likely more helpful than me making a couple very
barebones slides, since once I got up to speed I realized you had a lot of the
content in the doc.
The only other piece I could maybe see adding would be creating a 4 column table
(with the first being the accounting line item), and show a heavily redacted and
rounded version of the Revenue Detail Report - the 3 columns would be 1) numbers
from your worst year (or maybe second worst), 2) the average number across all
years, and 3) the numbers from your best year (or second best if you decide not
to use the worst for point 1 above). Basically to show a very simple table with
low/med/high scenarios of revenue and cost from past years.
Sorry again this took so long - I'm definitely a bit more swamped than expected,
so can't do too much more, but if you have any questions, please let me know,
and please do send me what you put together so I can do a quick scan and review.
Best,
Matthew

This is around the landing page where we intake businesses - initially most of
these will be businesses we talk to and we'll send them this link, but we'll
likely open it up as part of our public lead gen process or send it in an
automated drip to people who submit a business inquiry, as well as send along in
emails to provide context/follow up to our biz dev outreach to be transparent
about the process they'll have to go through to onboard
Form is attached - these will likely be businesses, funds, family offices,
investors, who are applying to be approved to hold large portions of dShares, as
well as maybe partners who are looking to work with us to build solutions into
their own applications using our dShares, smart contracts, etc
Any chance you could shoot to get me a very rough draft of this by Tuesday or so
(basic layout, draft of copy)?

---
date: 2023-09-14
to: Ethan Fassett <ethan@glystn.com>
subject: Re: In SF next Thurs/Fri
msg_id: 18a91d2dc60988f8
---

For the content itself-
-Looking at Logan Paul tweet, i would focus on sexier metrics. Total watch
time, total viewers, unless the sentiment difference is huge probably just
make it a row of a table. I would probably take more from head to head
style sports comparison graphics (halftime team summary stats in football
for instance).
-I would include social handles of the accounts you’re talking about -
definitely good to tag them for reshare
-I would distribute these posts on marketing forums that discuss these
events, maybe even Reddit if you can do it in a way that isn’t shilly
(which means having a very active Reddit account)
-I could see adding top keywords in comments/mentions to be an interesting
layer of insight
-I would definitely use event hashtags, as well as any releveant hashtags
or stuff around marketing analytics - hashtags still seem to be a good way
to find organic reach
-I have a feeling this sort of analytics content would do well on LinkedIn.
I know they have brutal pricing on their APIs, but this content could find
more quant/business minded creators there (and professional communities
could be an interesting type of niche to try and target)
-I would try and take an approach where each post/analysis has some similar
data points so you can aggregate into a comparison/ranking/macro analysis
post
-I would try and find an AI video production tool to try and piece these in
to quick videos - a lot of text on a lot of these graphics


For a ppc landing page validation test, my thoughts are:
-try and keep design and layout and even copy as similar as possible across
pages and ads
-run on at least 3 ad platforms with ideally $5k per platform
-plan 2 iterations of visual and copy to drill down into results (at least
1/4 of budget reserved for final drill down and gap filling analysis)
-use some sort of conversion signup to drive value from the ads/capture
leads, and send a survey at the end to allow all signups to see all value
propositions to confirm their choice/which is favorite in comparison
-run the test qualitatively with 15-20 friendlies
-make sure you have clean utm tagging to ensure easy to compare analytics
-every analytics platform has huge hole from adblockers and ad platforms
skew their metrics, so make sure to use ultimate CPA as most significant
indicator metric, and then add additional context with up funnel ad
impressions and clicks from each platform and Google analytics to try and
ad context
-I would look to quickly have some follow up to activate these signups into
discord, offer them a discount in product, etc. Maybe even have an
automated supplement form email send after signup asking them to pick from
all the ideas you’re testing (probably give a few days so it’s not asking
for too much info too quickly)


Happy to jump on a call whenever to discuss more. I’m heading to Michigan
early tomorrow morning for a wedding, and then bouncing around quite a bit
until I might be back in early November.

Hope this helps!



I also might suggest signing up for growthmentor.com (feel free to tell
Foti I sent you and he might give you a discount) - they have a lot of deep
subject matter experts on PPC (particularly not my biggest specialty), a/b
testing, CRO, hyper specific niche topics, B2B lead gen - I’ve referred at
least 5 solid people I know as mentors they’re and they usually give away
their time. Also solid content on various marketing topics.

---
date: 2023-09-13
to: Christine Leuthold <christine@inthenameofhope.com>
subject: Re: my film!  family update
msg_id: 18a90984c4332175
---

main feedback for the website/seo is there is too little text - google can only
return results for the words it sees on pages, ao i would maybe think about doig 
 some blog posts or editors room type content if you actually want people to
find you that way.
i'd  look at charity.water as a good model of marketing/fundraising, although
maybe a bit dated. they  were very oriented around subscriptions and reporting
 on what your money went to build.
If you could show a very direct dollar in-dolllarout flow of donations to
helping homeless, that might be a good markelting line to drive donations

date: 2023-09-13
to: Brian Leidel <bleidel@kbgrp.com>
subject: 2022 Filing Actions
msg_id: 18a9086acb3e6f97
---

Hi Brian,
Thanks for your help with my most recent filings. I wanted to make sure that I
am moving everything forward properly.
I received notifications of filings accepted for the FBAR, California, and IRS.
Was there a 4th return I should also be expecting a confirmation for?

In terms of payments, I'm a bit overwhelmed by all the docs I received. Am I
correct in thinking that in terms of payments due, the following is outstanding: * 
   $3,560 due by Sep 15 2023 via check to IRS - there is no possible way I can
   make this date, I am traveling and do not have access to a checkbook. Can I
   have my dad potentially send in the check? Is there any possible alternative
   payment method?
 * 
   $1,190 due Jan 16 2024 via check to IRS (is there any possible way to pay
   this digitally or via wire transfer? A check is quite inconvenient for me)
 * 
   I believe I saw there was a payment due to California as well?
 * 
   One of the payments due docs also appears to be a Voucher for $2,942 - is
   this a refund? Or is this additional payment I'm owed?
 * 
   Is there anything else in terms of payments to state or federal government?


Additionally, I believe I saw somewhere a bill for the actual filing services?
Was the one I paid a couple of months ago for the 2021 filing? If not, could you
please resend, as I have lost it in the flood of notifications.
In general I'm quite confused by next steps, so any summary or clarification
would be very helpful (and clarification if there is any way to pay these
digitally or remotely (or even through my dad), as I do not have access to a
checkbook until at least late October), particularly given that it appears the
deadline for payment is much sooner than I expected (I had thought these were
due in October, or are they just due a couple of weeks after whenever they're
filed?).
Thanks,
Matthew

date: 2023-09-03
to: gmuresan3@gmail.com
subject: Potential role
msg_id: 18a5cbc205b12c96
---

Hey man,

I just joined a new company, dinari.com, and we’re looking for someone with
front end/android app/smart contract experience (the engineers seem to
bounce around a bit and all are pretty full stack).

Basically 1:1 backed stock tokens (and generally very compliant approach),
launched outside of US, extremely strong founders, pretty remote team but
mainly west coast based.

Any interest?

Matthew

---
date: 2023-09-03
to: Ethan Fassett <ethan@glystn.com>
subject: Re: In SF next Thurs/Fri
msg_id: 18a5c805c7ae181c
---

Sure, anytime Tuesday or Wednesday afternoon should work for me

date: 2023-09-03
to: davisballard@gmail.com
subject: Dinari Davis
msg_id: 18a58d2cc31619d3

We are marketing securities, so cannot directly market then

Companies on this protocol rankings list are likely ones to draw from. We
will likely want to be more conservative in our messaging than all of them

Look up recent press on Google (Bloomberg, coindesk)

Matthew

---
date: 2023-08-27
to: jonathan saffari <jonathansaffari@gmail.com>
subject: Re: update
msg_id: 18a382da59ba922c
---

Hi Jon,
Sorry for the radio silence and general disorganization around this.
I've actually recently stepped out at Samba (as of last Thursday) - 2 week
notice period in the US snuck up on me a bit before I could tie up all lose 
ends such as giving you a better update :/. I would suggest doing any UI/app
related actions and probably pausing at that point. I'd use Tushar G as your day
to day point of contact for handing off designs, but Travis Gingerich (either
Travis@samba.tv or Travis.Gingerich@samba.tv) will be taking over the project
from an engineering (and lesser extent product) standpoint.
Sorry this didn't pan out as well (I'd be very surprised if this project
continues past September) - I'll likely reach out in the near future when I have
some more design work that needs to be done though, some potentially interesting
new projects coming up it seems
Hope all is well!
Matthew

Matthew

---
date: 2023-08-20
to: Christine Leuthold <ce3136@gmail.com>
subject: Re: An intro
msg_id: 18a13559af7964db
---

Hi Tom,

Sorry for the delay here - in a bit of a hectic period closing a house and
transitioning jobs.

Would be great to chat sometime and hear about what youre working on. My
new role looks like it will bring me a bit closer to fintech again.

Let me know some times that might work for you in the next week or two.

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2023-08-20
to: Ethan Fassett <ethan@glystn.com>
subject: Re: In SF next Thurs/Fri
msg_id: 18a1353c0c12e0e2
---

Hi Ethan,

No worries - I almost pinged you again just to check, although I had a
pretty hectic visit as it was. I should likely be back a couple of times in
the coming months.

I did want to give you a heads up that I gave my notice at Samba - I had
had my hours cut in half and made a contractor, and the project I was
working on was being under resourced already and presented with new cuts
(as well as company wide cuts).

I do have another gig I’m starting - dinari.com - hopefully if we see
enough growth we might be a good fit for glystn if you start servicing more
web3 companies/project communities in addition to influencer communities :)

Hope alls well with you, and let’s try and grab coffee or a drink next time
I’m in the city!

Matthew

Best,

Matthew

date: 2023-08-14
to: Brian Leidel <bleidel@kbgrp.com>
subject: Re: Additional UK Vang Capital filings and VA dividend clarification
msg_id: 189f47ea79b332d9
---

Hi Brian,

Re: Vang Capital dividends, I received this response from my UK
accountants: "We believe the difference is made up from the re-evaluation
reserve account not being included in the 2021-year end accounts.
Therefore, we have now included this adjustment in the prepared accounts
for 2022, which has altered the retained earnings to show what we believe
to be accurate figures aligning with Xero."

I do not believe this is correct/think there is some sort of error here
(not to mention don't understand which account they're referring to by
"reserve account", but they have not gotten back to me since Thursday.

When is the deadline for this information for the 2022 filing? Is there
anything else you need from me besides a response on this?

Best,

Matthew

*FAQs What's covered by my plan?*
Drops, spills, and cracked screens are covered from the date of purchase.
All other malfunction coverage begins after the manufacturer's warranty
ends.

*What's not covered by my plan?*
Your plan does not cover loss, theft, intentional damage, cosmetic damage
(unless it impedes functionality) and accessories that do not come in the
box with your covered product. Please see the *Terms and Conditions
<https://bturl.app/v1/1YNUEW>*for a complete list of what is not covered.

*When does my plan start?*
Your plan starts the day you purchase your product and continues for the
term indicated above under plan details.

*What about the manufacturer's warranty?*
Your plan does not replace the manufacturer's warranty, but provides
additional coverage during the manufacturer's warranty period. If your
product experiences a malfunction covered by the manufacturer's warranty,
please contact the manufacturer directly or ask us to connect you.

I think language around operating accounts covers what we’d need in capital
accounts.

most likely, so we can put that off til next year. For removal of a Member,
this is more in the scenario of bringing on a 3rd party investor and
wanting to subsequently remove them (or protecting ourselves from being
removed in that scenario), again I think that can wait til the refresh.

Apart from that, looks fine to me.  Let me know how you want to sign.

date: 2023-08-09
to: TeamAlpha <teamalpha@shieldaccountancy.co.uk>
subject: Re: Additional question re: Vang Capital for US Filing
msg_id: 189da8211bfe1576
---

Hi,

I don't understand what you mean by re-evaluation of reserve account not
being included. If it is the case of something that was missed/not
included/not done by Antonio, I believe you would have a better view on
this than I would. If that is the case, it is concerning that errors made
in 2021 are only coming to light today, and begs the question of if there
are additional ones from Antionio's time managing my accounts. Can you
please specify what you mean/which account you are referring to when you
say reserve account?

By backup information, can you specify what you are looking for beyond the
spreadsheet I had provided previously? This is the most detailed,
consolidated view that I have on investments made and current values/values
over time for Vang Capital, and was created in collaboration with Antonio
for what he had said were the requirements for my filing. If there is
additional information needed, could you please specify? Moreover, almost
all of these gains are unrealized, so I am unclear as to how Vang Capital
has made a profit that is taxable (unless calculations are made for
individual companies I invest in, or there is taxation on unrealized gains).

Thanks,

Matthew

---
date: 2023-08-09
to: Gabriel Otte <gabe@dinari.com>
subject: Re: Dinari Offer
msg_id: 189da7a11b46ac08
---

Hi Gabe,

Thanks for this, and excited to be working with you :)


The spreadsheet attached is generally pretty self explanatory, very useful,
and appreciate the transparency (as well as having numbers to look at). I
do want to clarify:

   - In row 2 ("Your Salary + Bonus") - this number does not look like it
   is including the two 50k bonuses you've mentioned in your email, correct?
   - It looks like share count is for 4 years? I assume this is 4 year
   vest, 1 year cliff?
   - What is the exercise window on the options?


I think we're thinking along the same lines for bonus structure. I could
see even maybe having a few liquidity based milestones (i.e. $10k at 1M,
another $10k at 2.5M, etc), in parallel to a few volume/userbase/affiliate
partner based milestones - likely with a larger portion of the total bonus
pool allocated for the liquidity based bonuses. Maybe we can set something
now with a term that if we both agree, we can change the target metric if
we should realize there is another more strategically aligned
"not-liquidity" metric we want to focus on driving.


Just to clarify, when you say 100k new orders, you're referring to 100k
cumulative trades through the platform (minus any that have been made
already), not daily orders, correct?


I did also want to ask, what had you envisioned as the title for this role?


Let me know what you think, or if it'd make more sense to hop on a call -
I'm quite flexible today. Or do let me know if you need anything else from
me!

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2023-08-09
to: Dean Eisner <dean.eisner@gmail.com>
subject: (no subject)
msg_id: 189da61dcbeea50a
---

Hi Ashwin and Charles,

Given the changes over the past few months to our approach to resourcing
and prioritization of this project, and in light of the recent changing of
the terms of my role, I unfortunately need to give my 2 weeks notice.

Happy to discuss this further, or to maintain some flexibility in the
timing of my exit. However, since April, I have been raising a red flag
about not having an engineering lead on this project. Travis seems
extremely capable, but this represents one of 4 projects for him. Without a
full time engineering lead, I see this project having drastically smaller
chance of success (I would estimate from 50% chance of success originally
down to 20%). Coupled with a new high pressure timeline, a reduction of the
time I can work on this, and significantly less attention being paid to
this project in general, I would estimate chance of success has further
dropped from 20% to under 10%.

I would have liked to explore other options (cutting out junior engineers
to hire a senior one, spinning out and raising VC funding, etc), but at
this point I need to prioritise my occupational and financial stability
(particularly in light of closing on a house I need to make sure I can pay
for).

Let me know anything you need from me in this process. In the meantime, I
will create handoff documentation, as well as finalise all sprint planning
through the end of September.

Best,

Matthew

date: 2023-08-07

subject: Additional question re: Vang Capital for US Filing
msg_id: 189d144b1e3ab7a1
---

Hi,
My US accountant had an additional question regarding Vang Capital statements
for my US filing. If there's any way I could get a response in the next day or
two, I should have it in time for my filing at the beginning of next week.
"There is a decrease in retained earnings of £ 34,448 ($44,529 US Dollars) which
is not shown in the financial statements. This company has not previously paid
dividends. Were there dividends for this amount paid 50% (MATTHEW NOTE: I don't
believe there were any dividends paid)?   Or, has there been a change in prior
year(s) balances or to retained earnings?"


Also, in looking at the Vang Capital statements in more detail, I am a little
surprised to see an income as opposed to loss reported. Is this because revenue
is calculated for each individual investment? If it were done at aggregate level
across all investments, I would have expected "losses" from making new
investments would outweigh revenues from returns.
Thanks,
Matthew

to do final review/get back with any additional questions.

Matthew

date: 2023-07-28
to: "Belk, Beth" <BBelk@ibcp.com>
subject: Re: 1228 25th St-Purchase
msg_id: 1899d7cf85e88782
---

Hi Beth,

Thanks for the clarification. I have requested $85,000 to be withdrawn from
the trust to my Independent account. This usually takes them about a day or
two, but might take a day or two longer with this being a new account they
have not transferred to before. I will let you know when this has been
processed and provide transaction statements.

In the more mid-term, it would be good to figure out what the minimum
amount that needs to be stored in this account is. I have made very good
returns in the market this year, and at least could see above 5% risk-free
interest in a savings account with that money, so would prefer to maximize
my returns from that and minimize the amount funds that are held with 0%
return in a checking account. However, my main priority at this point is
getting this purchase closed.

I did also want to see if we could get a copy of the appraisal report.

Let me know if there's anything else you need from me.

Best,

Matthew

for?

another family member who uses them as their lawyer regularly call them,
but have not gotten a response. Given the time of year, it is possible that
they are on vacation.

I will try the lawyer again tomorrow, but want to ensure this doesn't delay
closing, so wanted to see if there might be some alternative paths. Could
one of the following options potentially work?

   1. Withdraw a certain amount of funds from the trust to my Truist
   account (or Independent account)
   2. My mother has offered me a gift for the purchase of a home of up to
   $100,000. I had not planned to use it for this purchase, but could do so
   for this purchase instead if this would expedite this process.
   3. Maybe the attached document suffices?


Let me know if the doc or any of the above alternatives work, and in the
meantime/otherwise, I will keep chasing the lawyer.

Matthew

date: 2023-07-26
to: Brian Leidel <bleidel@kbgrp.com>
subject: Tax forms for trust
msg_id: 18993b50224defe1
---

Hi Brian,

Hope all is well with you.

As we’ve discussed briefly, I’m purchasing a house in Michigan, and am on a
bit of a wild goose chase to collect some last minute requests for docs for
the mortgage brokers.

Specifically, I need documentation (a certificate of trust) of the
irrevocable trust that I was given access to after my grandmother passed
last year that Montag Wealth manages. I had contacted them, and they
suggested I reach out to the lawyer who set up the trust, as well as you
regarding tax filings.

Do you have any tax documentation, filings, a tax ID, etc related to this?
Anything is useful, as the bank agent is being very vague about what is
required.

Thanks!

Matthew

being asked by the mortgage broker to provide a certificate of trust that I
was given access to 2 or so years ago.

provide any more detail on what this should include).

Is this something you can help with? Thanks!

Matthew
