date: 2023-07-26
to: "Belk, Beth" <BBelk@ibcp.com>
subject: Re: 1228 25th St-Purchase
msg_id: 1898fbdea5b3959a
---

Hi Beth,

Thanks for your email.

As a reminder, I was completely off the grid from July 19th until today. I
also was not informed of any other documents being outstanding apart from a
vague notification email from the portal which went to my spam folder, so
anything you can do to inform me of newly required items in the future
would be very helpful to ensure I can get them to you quickly.

I have submitted some additional items, however there are some remaining
who's instructions are unclear or that I have questions about - I have
listed them below. Please provide additional information so that I can get
these back to you as soon as possible.

   - Letter of Explanation for move to MI - I am unsure why this is
   necessary, as I am not changing my tax/employment residency to Michigan,
   and will remain a tax resident in Florida and maintain my employment there.
   I believe my employment contract also states that I am a remote employee.
   This is not an issue to get, but will likely take some time, as the HR
   department in my company is notoriously slow. *Is this necessary to
   still do?*


   - Checking account setup - I have not yet received a call from Victoria
   to do this. If she had called in the last few days, I did not have
   cell/internet connection. *Please give me a number I can contact her at
   if this is urgent.*


   - Certificate of Trust - I have asked my wealth manager for this, and
   they have generally been quite timely so I will likely receive it and
   upload it tomorrow. *If there are other documents you will need related
   to the trust, please inform me so I can ask for them all in a single email
   from them.*


   - Hazard Insurance - this is the first I have heard about needing this,
   so we will have to start to process of connecting to an agent and obtaining
   a quote, which we will do tomorrow


I also do not believe I have received a copy of the appraisal - *can you
please send this when you can?*

In the future, please continue to cc Nick (added to this email) on any
correspondence, as he can help move things forward if I am unavailable (as
I often am during the day due to my job). Please let me know if there is
anything else you foresee needing, as the sooner I can get started on
chasing it, the sooner you will have it.

Thanks,

Matthew

---
date: 2023-07-17
to: George Muresan <gmuresan3@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Startup Roadmap Outline Template?
msg_id: 18961971b62421c4
---

Sounds good - I took a quick glance but will look in more detail ahead of
our call tomorrow.

Throwing words on a page is a good place to start - this is generally a
process of reducing down from more text to less, and then iteration of nit
picky language and trying to create useful visualizations (at least the
deck content/production side).

For the content, there are 8 or so pretty standard slides in most decks,
and then 2-4 more variable ones (still usually from a set of 10-15 typical
types of slides) - you probably already have 75% of the info for them here
in the doc, and another 85% if you keep fleshing out.

The other pieces are more check-box/VC language/pulling some metrics about
market size and creating revenue/cost models. All stuff that's pretty
straightforward and I can point you towards.

But the art of a pitch is really the iterative process of getting feedback,
figuring out language resonates/gets people's interest, and focusing down
on those pieces to create a story that makes investors think this is an
opportunity that's better than the one's they've heard - that just takes
time and pitching.

Looking forward to catching up!

---
date: 2023-07-17
to: George Muresan <gmuresan3@gmail.com>
subject: Re: Startup Roadmap Outline Template?
msg_id: 189615f88b3fc564
---

subject: Charles and ash email
msg_id: 1895d64bb94c42df
---

Hi Charles and Ashwin,

I wanted to provide an email update as I prepare to head out for holiday,
particularly given some previously raised red flags/gray areas that may
stall somewhat in my absence or likely need your attention to more forward.

1) product/design (v3 implementation, next steps?)
2) engineering threads (viewership data, tv device connection
3) Travis as point of contact
4) other work streams (streaming data development costing, business line
definition and revenue scenario modelling,
5) high level approach to project/integration - understanding who should be
looping in on comms, mid term plan for engineering,


General flag that we have been operating suboptimally. [[ENGINEERING
SUGGESTIONS]]

probably the longest and least internet-connected holiday I have taken in
my career (so excuse


Additional details for charles:
-PRD/FRD
-time in sf
-


<<<<Add spin out via venture capital as option>>>>

date: 2023-07-14

subject: Re: Following up on Vang Advisory annual statement for US tax filing
msg_id: 189555cc1aafafee

Hi Kevin,

This needed to be asked earlier. I am in California, so my morning (now) is
likely too late for you. This has been the case for quite some time, and I
have asked at least 3 times if a call should be scheduled because that is
what we had done in previous years.

There is no longer any flexibility in planning here. I will stay up until
after Midnight this coming Sunday to ensure we can make this call happen. A
safer plan of action is for these to be sent along as text, but as I have
been mentioning for the past month, we are past the point where there is
time for this inefficient back and forth.

This is really becoming a very serious issue. An IRS audit in the US is
extremely costly and time consuming, and I am now being put in a position
by you and your team where this is extremely likely to happen.

This really is extremely disappointing, particularly after me having to
have chased this 5+ times and your assurances that it would be completed
weeks ago, as well as by today.

Matthew

wrote:

have word back to you by the end of the week (and as mentioned, do want to
work with you if it’s not too huge of a difference in numbers).

of rate.

Matthew

date: 2023-07-13
to: Nektarios Liolios <nektarios@radish.credit>
subject: Re: Radish Intro
msg_id: 1894d41227a680e4
---

Hi all,

Was great to talk and hear a bit more about Radish. It's quite apparent you
have a very deep knowledge and complementary skill set around this, so
forgive some likely rather elementary questions from my side - I do always
enjoy the chance to get an inside look of an industry while investing :)

I did have a bit of influx of friends and referrals coming to me with
opportunities the last couple of weeks, so it would be helpful if we could
stick closer to 5k. That being said, I realize that I've likely been
speaking in USD and you in GBP, so if it makes a particular difference, we
could do GBP on this.

   - When are you launching a product to market? Any particular
   order/cadence/strategy driving which community organizations/use cases to
   target first, or generally partner driven?
   - How do you see attracting your first group of users? What resources
   are planned for that?
   - Does Radish take on any risk with any of the loans it facilitates? Do
   lenders not really see it as risk b/c they see it as part of CSR budget?
   - Do you have any financial modelling you can share to get a feel for
   what breakeven, growth, revenue potential might look like at various scales?
   - If you have any stakeholder/investor update emails, I'd love to see
   one or two (but know it might be a bit early to be doing that)
   - What do you see as ideal exit paths?


Also, a random thought in going through the deck, but I imagine there's
some pretty interesting consumer research around looking at how communities
influence buying decisions/drive purchase referrals, and I imagine there's
even some specifically that compares financial and other complex products
with basic/generic products.

I'll be off the grid July 19-26 or so, but happy to pick this up before or
after then, and looking forward to hearing more!

Best,

Matthew

ready to launch… I mean I should actually be fine, but definitely up in the
air. Likely by end of year if things aren’t going really well they’ll axe
my project.

I’m also looking for stuff so I decent chance I leave before they let me go
(especially if I’d have to stay until exit or sale as opposed to a new
funding round to get value from my vesting shares - my original plan was to
start looking in Sept because I assumed I would keep the shares if I left
once they vested)

date: 2023-07-07
to: matthew.eisner@samba.tv
subject: Hybrid policy to clarify
msg_id: 1892ef12da331d4f
---

Appreciate the broader communication round hybrid policy. Is 3 or 4 days
the policy for product?

Do I need to move to a location where I am within 50 miles? Seems trivial
seeming the only person I directly coordinate with this point outside of
India full time is Charles, and rest are in SF, UK, NYC, etc, and I can
make a point of visiting all of those (esp if there is some financial
support for this)? How firm is this policy? How disadvantaged will I be
outside of the extra effort of coordination will I be if I am not on
locations 3+ days a week

Do we no longer believe remote decentralised work is an approach we want to
build for in terms of a web3/blockchain solution?

I can attempt to be in an office when in LA, SF, or other cities I happen
to be in that have offices. I have made a point to do this anyways, but can
make a more concerted one. However, outside of NYC, LDN, Poland, SF, im not
sure which offices I actually have a working point of contact with, or
which would end up simply being me networking with our internal team, but
ultimately with staff who are in very peripheral areas to the ones I am
focused in or currently or am planning to coordinate with in the near
future.

I had mentioned a plan to set up some roots in Detroit as well as have some
in Florida (but Sarasota, not Miami, where my current contract is based).
Is there any plans to set up on site offices in these locations? Ashwin had
mentioned maybe setting up an entity in Michigan, but I’m not sure if this
included an office location (although office space is very cheap there)

date: 2023-06-30
to: matthew.eisner@samba.tv
subject: Charles email
msg_id: 1890e2def7594fcc
---

I like the smoking pan analogy you used, and certainly see, especially
after seeing all the other smoking pan priorities in core data and
revenue-dependencies in our roadmap call today from the top level business
unit.

From the perspective of leading this project, the kitchen has been on fire
for 2-3 months now, which has been, to put it very mildly, a stressful
experience. I have raised in every 1:1 with CJS and weekly management call
we’ve had in that period of time. I saw a potential fire extinguisher in
making offers for Brandon’s replacement, although am now worried that is on
hold. More broadly, it does sound like you are offering me a fire
extinguisher, but I am not completely free in where that is coming from. I
can sit tight where you need me to, and am more than happy to provide
anything I can to help facilitate your help, but as much clarity and heads
up as you can provide as to next steps, expectation, where I should focus,
or how I can help you help me is extremely appreciated.

To that end, I wanted to follow up on a few threads as promised from our
1:1 Weds:

-I have followed up with Brandon on getting code repos, PR requests, etc.
I’ve told him I will chase him again Monday, as I want to be respectful of
the fact that he is having some relatively significant family health
issues. If we haven’t heard back by Monday, I will chase again, but it may
be worth having Ashwin give him a quick nudge at that point. I will of
course keep following up with Brandon though.

-I have collected what materials I can here

-I also have summarised current active workstreams below:
—Engineering
—Product
—Recruitment
—Design
—GTM
—Partners
—Blockchain


Also following up from today, I’ve linked the deck from the call here. I
will also:

-Go through and backfill Jira (we had kept it pretty streamlined up until
now of things in our 4-8 week future, but easy enough transfer some of

-Onboard to Monday - Ankit has shared a board with me. I have never used it
before, but I don’t imagine it’s too tricky. That being said, if there are
highly specific/unique/intricate ways we have it set up, my entries may be
somewhat messier than most people’s.

-I am not sure what to do from there - should I touch base with you and
Jaya at that point to try and get my items into the larger roadmap?


I do want to flag that I am somewhat unclear of what to prioritise and how
to ensure I am using my time efficiently. I have spent at least a half day
this week working on decks that likely are never going to be seen, and it
seems my time would have been far more effective just going ahead and
entering my information into Monday instead of preparing short presentation
(I certainly would have run under allocated time). I’d particularly like to
highlight that on our call Weds, I had asked if I should be going .

I certainly understand there is a learning curve in onboarding, and there
are bigger fish to fry organisationally, but I am already spending 2/3rds
of my time trying to cover for a role that is very outside of my skill set
while we look for a replacement, so I am urgently trying to figure out how
to use my time as effectively possible during this period of wider
organziational change.

---
date: 2023-06-30
to: Jerome Dixon <jerome@residentialrome.com>
subject: Re: Following up on 25th Street duplex

asking, with some favourable contingencies I think for the seller. But it
sounded like that’s where we’d have to come in to be competitive really.
Between us, I think we were somewhat aided by Randall working at the same
agency as the selling agent, although I’m not sure and may be mixing up
terminology.

Code repos
PRs

jira

Priorities and Workstreans
-Tech

—Activate points system

-Non Tech

—App QA
—Competitor research and UX tear downs
—Bluesky TV UX features and UI
—recruitment of appropriate engineering team (pending budgets)
—Revenue pilots

Matthew

Matthew

date: 2023-06-25
to: John Montag <johnmontag@montagwealth.com>
subject: Real estate purchase
msg_id: 188f307488fd0cb0
---

Hi John,

Hope you've been doing well!

I've been looking at making one or two real estate purchases, and my dad
said I should reach out to you, particularly around variable vs fixed rate
loans, but also in general.

To give a quick summary, I have been looking at buying 1 or 2 properties in
Detroit, with the vision that I would spend 2-3 months per year there in
not winter, and rent the other units/maybe a bedroom in my unit to cover or
come close to covering mortgage and expenses.

I've put an offer in on a duplex for just under $400k with a close friend
who has 2 other properties he rents and manages (with him as 60% stake and
me as 40%). He's been pretty heavily leaning to fixed rate loan, but is
open minded. My and my dad's intuition is more variable. Also for some
context, I would not plan to live in this property.

Assuming this closes (or doesn't), I would look to buy another duplex or
condo for myself assuming the deal closes (which sounds likely), or make an
offer on a currently way overpriced but has spent a half year on the market
7-unit property (which would likely have to be largely in cash or investor
financed) in partnership with another friend who is a property developer
and currently rents in the building which I would live in (there are a lot
of moving parts to sort through for this last one).

I will have no particular rush to make this second purchase, although it
would be nice to establish a bit of a home base and have somewhere to put
my things, but also I have heavy travel planned through early next year,
and would not be able to homestead the second property for loan purposes
for a decent amount of time. I did a bunch of viewings in June and am
pretty locked in to the market, and will go back in September with a little
more urgency, and if I don't find anything then, will likely go back again
in Spring 2024 with significant urgency to make a purchase. If I didn't
close on the duplex with my friend, I would probably definitely be looking
to close on something in September.

Happy to jump on a call if that makes sense, or any resources/information
you could point me to would be hugely appreciated!

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2023-06-25
to: Brian Leidel <bleidel@kbgrp.com>
subject: Re: Acorns and Robinhood 5489
msg_id: 188f2f780c41834e
---

My intuition is we would likely be reinvesting rental revenue into
management, mortgage payments, taxes, contingency budget, etc, and then
periodically pay off capital contributions for the initial purpose if the
contingency budget started to grow, and then pay back full capital
contribution and distribute any gains after the sale.

Is there a major tax implication for either? I imagine any profits paid out
are taxed similarly whether now or in the future.

date: 2023-06-20
to: "R. Philip Lockwood" <rphiliplockwood@gmail.com>
subject: Following up on 4766 purchase
msg_id: 188d657a9dc9efc3
---

Hi Phil,
Thanks for your time last week, and excited to potentially be partnering up on
this :) 
To consolidate my thoughts and put my questions in one place, I’d say these are
the areas where I need a bit more detail to be comfortable moving forward (I
know you’re probably working on the first two or three, and I’ll likely be
getting a pro-forma partnership agreement from Jerome as well): * Finance (incl
   detailed rental cash flow, low/med/high projections, debt carrying cost,
   taxes, insurance, schedule plan for vacancies, time model of when rehabs/rent
   increases can be performed)

 * Improvement and running costs (incl tax and detailed maintenance/improvement
   estimates)

 * Partnership agreement, governance, decision making,
   dissolution/buy-out/early-exit/wind-down, incentive alignment
   (maintenance/improvement fees, management fees, owner-rental rates, etc),
   other obligations/commitments, and any other due diligence

 * Closing/sale process


Also, given that it sounds like we’d need to do all cash up front, and to avoid
a 50/50 split, I think I’d prefer to go in for anywhere from a 33% to 40% stake
of ownership. It’s not a dealbreaker for me if not, but I might need to crunch a
few more numbers and ensure I have the line of family credit that I think I do
if  I need to do 50%.
Additionally, I had mentioned a few interesting mixed use spaces in London - the
ones that are probably most notable/successful to me are: The Hoxton Hotel,  
Boxpark, Tobacco Dock, Village Underground  (the tube cars on top are coworking
offices).
Best,
Matthew

---
date: 2023-06-20
to: Jerome Dixon <jerome@residentialrome.com>
subject: Following up on 25th Street duplex
msg_id: 188d64dddfac37a1
---

Hi Jerome,
Thanks for the call last week, and the additional information about the 25th
Street property, and excited to potentially be partnering up on this! 
I’m definitely interested in moving forward, and wanted to map the questions I
had so that we can keep the process moving (although I think you had mentioned
you’re already working on getting materials that would cover the first 3): * 
   Finance (incl loan process for two-part application, detailed rental cash
   flow (incl low/med/high projections, debt carrying cost, taxes, insurance,
   schedule plan for vacancies, etc), etc)

 * Improvement and running costs (incl tax/NEZ and detailed maintenance
   estimates)

 * Partnership agreement, governance, decision making,
   dissolution/buy-out/early-exit/wind-down, incentive alignment
   (maintenance/improvement fees, management fees, owner-rental rates, etc),
   other obligations/commitments, and any other due diligence

 * Closing/sale process with two buying parties

 * I also did want to check if you’d be open to having a third party (my two
   friends I mentioned who have 4 properties had actually sent me the property a
   while ago, but are a bit short on capital at the moment). This is not at all
   a dealbreaker (I could certainly see how this might add unnecessary
   complication), and we could also look to cut them in later, but another way
   to avoid decision making lockup, and wanted to check and see what your
   thinking was on this.



Also, to avoid a 50/50 split and free up some capital for another purchase to
use as my residence, I think I’d prefer to go in for anywhere from a 33% to 40%
stake of ownership. It’s not a dealbreaker for me if not, but I might need to
crunch a few more numbers to ensure I’d still be able to make another purchase
for myself or the Commonwealth property with Phil (likely closer to 33% ideally
if the latter goes forward).
Let me know if you have any other thoughts, what next steps/actions are, and
send along pro-forma agreements, financial models, etc when you can.
Best,
Matthew

date: 2023-06-19
to: Randall Fogelman-OCONNOR <randall@oconnordetroit.com>
subject: Property search update
msg_id: 188d5fe40acb5883
---

Hi Randall,
I hope you're doing well. I wanted to provide you with an update (and
consolidate the information from my previous email).
Firstly, I've had discussions with both Phil regarding the Commonwealth 7-unit
property, and Jerome regarding the 25th Street duplex. Both options seem
promising, and I plan to further explore them next week. In case I don't decide
to proceed with either of them, I intend to start making lowball offers
relatively quickly, around 2 weeks from now. A friend who runs a fund/lending
company recommended having representation for these deals, should they
materialize.
Secondly, here are the likely scenarios I'm considering: * Partnering with Phil
   to purchase 4766 and residing in one of the rooms.

 * Partnering with Jerome to purchase the 25th Street property and looking for
   another property for myself to live in within the next 3-9 months.

 * Purchasing both 4766 and the 25th Street properties in separate partnerships,
   with stakes of around 40% in each, while residing in a room at 4766

 * Not purchasing either 4766 or the 25th Street property and instead focusing
   on acquiring a duplex, house, or condo within the next 3 months. If it's a
   condo or small house, I would consider making a second purchase within the
   subsequent 3-9 months.


Thirdly, I've thoroughly reviewed all relevant properties on Zillow and will
continue to monitor new listings on a weekly basis, and update my spreadsheet.
Could you please advise me on the best way to gather more information, such as
video walkthroughs, for the properties I'm interested in? I may be able to
arrange for Nick to assist me, but I'd like to explore other options as well.
Alternatively, I can plan to handle this when I'm back in town for most of
September. These are the properties in particular I am most interested in
learning more in at the moment, but this can wait a week or two until I've
hashed through the details of 4766 and 25th Street (in addition to anything
higher than medium and "No" for viewed in the spreadsheet):
 * 8126 E Vernor
 * 3066 Belmont
 * 7874 Van Dyke Pl
 * 1501 Atkinson St
 * 89 Hague St
 * 242 Melbourne St
   
 * 1200 Porter
   
 * 1965 Orleans #20
 * 5201 Commonwealth #11
 * 1466 W Philadeplhia


Lastly, I'm in the process of getting a pre-approval from Schwab/Rocket. My
financial advisor at Schwab suggested checking their rates, which are expected
to be approximately half a percent lower.

Please let me know if there's anything I may have overlooked, if you have any
immediate thoughts, and what the best approach would be for the next steps. I
will follow up with more urgency once I have more information on the
Commonwealth and 25th Street properties, either to get your input on the deals
or to explore alternative options.
Best,
Matthew

Hi Adrian,

Hope all is well! What have you been up to recently?

I’ve been working with a partner from back in my Startupbootcamp days, who
then spent 4-5 years in SE Asia doing venture building and corporate
innovation consultancy for Rainmaking to set up a blockchain fund and
venture studio. He mentioned to me today that we just sent a proposal to
Abhay at Moonpay to set up a venture building studio, and I was curious if
it was on your radar at all.

Also, I’ll be in London Sept 19-26 if you might be around - I’ll follow up
closer to then.

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2023-06-14
to: Susie Edwards <Susie.Edwards@huntington.com>
subject: Re: Huntington Pre-approval
msg_id: 188ba74e517dfd98
---

Hi Susie,

I'm traveling again. Thanks for the pre-approval!

The tours went somewhat well - at this point, it seems like my process will
be:

   - Determine in the next week or two if the 4766 Commonwealth purchase in
   partnership with Phil Lockwood, who lives in the building/runs a
   development company is something I want to move forward with. It sounds
   like financing around this would be difficult, but it is possible I could
   go largely or entirely cash if needed. If I did this, I would probably not
   be looking to make any other property purchases


   - Determine ASAP if I will look to move forward on the 25th Street
   duplex in partnership with Jerome Dixon, who is an agent and runs a
   management company. This would likely be a pure investment/rental property
   that I would not plan to live in. It sounds like it will likely move quite
   quickly


   - Unless I make the 4766 Commonwealth purchase, I will look to purchase
   a duplex, condo, or house to live in myself and ideally rent a room (and if
   multi-unit, unit) in. Pretty much as soon as I have finished vetting the
   Commonwealth property (if I do not move forward) or making the purchase on
   25th Street duplex (regardless of if I do or do not move forward), I will
   look to start making low-ball offers on a few places I saw, as well as
   continuing to look for new options. This will likely start in early July. I
   will be back in town for 2-3 weeks in early/mid September, and if I haven't
   found a place by then, I will likely be looking and making offers with a
   bit more urgency.


It does sound like increasing the cash portion of an offer could make it
more competitive, or lead to a lowering of price, so I will likely consider
that, which I imagine could change the terms of the loan.

Let me know if there's anything else!

Best,

Matthew

The transfer to Vang Advisory was to transfer shares in a startup I

discount rate to Vang Capital. If there is any documentation I need to
create, or another way this should be managed, please let me know. I've

Matthew

Hey Matthew.

Susie

Internal Use

*From:* Matthew Eisner <matteisn@gmail.com>
*Sent:* Thursday, June 8, 2023 4:02 PM
*To:* Lori Campbell <Lori.L.Campbell@huntington.com>
*Cc:* Susie Edwards <Susie.Edwards@huntington.com>
*Subject:* Re: Huntington Pre-approval



*Huntington Security Checkpoint:** External email. Please make sure you
trust this source before clicking links or opening attachments.*

Hi Lori,



Thanks for this and your time on the phone yesterday - I will forward along
the statement momentarily.



If you could also please confirm how I might go about getting pre-approved
for a 7 unit, 2 building property purchase (4766 Commonwealth), that would
also be aprpeciated. There is a potential I would go 50/50 (or somewhere
close to that) with a friend who currently lives in the building, which I
imagine changes/potentially complicates the deal/loan somewhat. I have
heard from a broker that it would be very difficult to get a loan on this
however (although there is a chance we could look at splitting it into two
properties, permitting allowing), and in general there is a lot of
complexity to be hashed through around this, although it is becoming a
serious consideration.



I also did a viewing today of 1346 Bagley , which is definitely the most
interesting condo/townhome I’ve seen thus far. It would be good to maybe
use this as a more likely case of the transaction that might take place. I
also would likely consider buying a second unit as an investment/rental in
the same building, but would likely do that on a longer time scale (unless
there were significant financial upsides in either the purchase price or
loan as a result of batching them).



Also, I did want to mention, I could pretty easily put more than 25% down
if it would make sense from a cash flow and interest standpoint. I would
need to do a bit of modelling on my side however to make sure it made sense
for me.



Let me know if you need anything else from me!



Matthew

date: 2023-06-11
to: Christine Leuthold <christine@inthenameofhope.com>
subject: Re: sarah said she could set it up.... maybe then you help w SEO?   Fwd: would you be able to help w this?  Fwd: Hope website landing page?
msg_id: 188ac31306093ea7
---

Sure - I can do it with whatever you use really

I would not have a qr code on the landing page. A qr code is used to send
someone on their mobile phone to a website. The people on your website are
already there, and ones on their phone won’t be able to scan a qr code
(which 70%+ of your visitors will probably be on mobile). Just have a
donation box on the landing page

On Sat, Jun 10, 2023 at 19:35 Christine Leuthold <

---
date: 2023-06-09
to: Randall Fogelman-OCONNOR <randall@oconnordetroit.com>
subject: Re: FW: Invest in Detroit's Most Desirable Neighborhoods: 5 Impressive Multifamily Buildings Now Available
msg_id: 188a211344b782d7
---

Hi Randall,

First of all, happy birthday, have a good long weekend, and please don't
bother reading all of this until Monday - nothing urgent I don't think. And
apologies for the general length of this email, but thought it's probably
useful to see my thought process/context.


Thanks very much for these, and for meeting yesterday. Glad to be working
together.

As I mentioned yesterday, I did do a set of viewings with Jerome today, and
informed him I'd be working with you. He was very understanding, although
of course briefly tried to pitch himself, but is happy to send me more
stuff if he sees anything, but won't be actively looking. From my
standpoint, there is likely quite a bit of family headache if I burn (or am
perceived by the Schostaks to have burned) Jerome in any way, so if there
was a scenario that you and I closed on a property he initially showed me,
I'd likely throw him a menial finders fee (less than $1500), largely to
avoid the that (and have pencil plans to take him out to dinner in
September to thank him for his time). That being said, I expect to be
sourcing the majority of properties from here on out myself via Zillow, or
via referrals from Nick, you, etc.

He also had been planning to do video walkthroughs with me of the duplexes
at Van Dyke Place, Field, and 25th Street from pretty early last week
because they are not available for viewing until next week. I'm happy to do
the Van Dyke Place and Field properties with him, and then do another with
you if I'm serious about them if that's a better use of your time. Or also
happy to just do them with you. My thinking on those two however was
they're a bit farther out/more east than I'd ideally like. But certainly
open to them, especially at the price point.

For the 25th Street property, Jerome mentioned he thinks it will move quite
fast and is a very good deal, and it does seem somewhat solid as a pure
investment/rental unit, although I would likely consider this much more as
an investment/rental than a property I would inhabit for more than 3 months
or so (and maybe not even that). As we were wrapping up today, he mentioned
that we would be very interested in going 50/50 on it with me. Obviously I
would do quite a bit of due diligence and ensure quite a few
rails/safegaurds were in place before moving forward, but this does
potentially sound very interesting as a low risk and low capital
opportunity to quickly dip a toe into the market (which is desirable to
contrast with some of my higher risk early stage startup investing with no
true asset backing I do a lot of). I personally am less bearish than a lot
of what I'm hearing about the market (and particularly real estate), so
would ideally like to start getting in sooner rather than later, but will
still be very conservative/risk averse as I do. I'll also likely talk to
Nick about the 25th Street property (actually already have lightly via
text). Either way, I do see significant added value/convenience for me in
partnering with someone with experience in the space, particularly for a
property that is more maintenance/management intensive, whether that's with
Phil, Nick, or maybe even Jerome. All do say definitely interested in this
property, with or without a partner, but potentially some advantages if I
can navigate the complexity/there's a good fit.

For both the 4766 buildings and the 25th Street duplex, I will be doing
quite a bit of DD and research next week, as well as having follow up with
both Phil and Jerome by the end of the week, to see if either make sense,
or if I should focus more on making a purchase independently. Regardless,
given both of their superior knowledge to me of this space, I imagine it
would still make sense to have you represent my interests were these
transactions to move forward? And for the 25th Street transaction, were
that to move forward, I would likely still be looking at other duplexes or
even single unit condos or houses, likely at a higher price point, to
partially rent (or rent a unit in the case of duplex) and share the unit
with a friend so that I could use it personally for storage/as a home base.

For the E Vernor property in the email below is also certainly interesting,
especially at the ppsqft, but I imagine I'd need to hire a management
company to take care of it (not a huge issue), and again sounds like
similarly located to Van Dyke and Field (although sounds like the Field
location is maybe slightly closer in than the other two). Would definitely
be good for me to try and get for a feel for these areas - I'll do some
digging on Google Street View and Maps. The Belmont property seems like it
may be an even better fit, although I'll have to do some digging on the map
to understand exactly what's around it/where it is in context of the other
places we saw yesterday - it sounds like somewhat closer (without having
any real understanding of the layout of Detroit).

I've also updated/reorganized my spreadsheet
<https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1M1BNgCblTGcWSl3eyrrKXxNXNTF_NayYVSjlZODPVDk/edit#gid=155769907>
- I think the only real changes were downgrading a few places I looked at,
and that I've somewhat confirmed my intrigue in the listings on Brooklyn
Ave, Bagley, E Grand. I'd probably want to start making low ball offers on
these once I virtually view any other or eliminate through research the
other interesting units (mostly multi-units) on my list (anything currently
listed as Medium or higher in the Fit column I haven't viewed yet), and
look a bit deeper into the 4766 and 25th Street properties/partnerships,
maybe as soon as end of next week if you think that makes sense, but most
likely by the end of the month. I'd also be interested in looking at what
you would see as slightly less expensive versions of these condos (i.e.
needing a bit more work/not as modern).

In general, I should have a much more concise and narrow picture by end of
next week, as well as some feedback/info from Susie on my pre-approval
application. I'll plan to compile that info and send along then. If there's
anything else I should be considering, or any feedback you have on what
I've mentioned above, please do let me know.

Thanks for all your help!

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2023-06-09
to: Christine Leuthold <christine@inthenameofhope.com>
subject: Re: How did it check out?      Also document download...
msg_id: 188a19ef359ad26b
---

Are you referring to the home?

It was good, lots of strings attached - still need to align on partnership,
financing, and operations/improvement. Should have a feel of partnership
next week.

There’s also another duplex that one of the agents (the Schostaks agent)
I’m working with and who has a property management company is willing to go
50/50 or so in with me on. It’s very cheap, and could be another
interesting way for me to dip my feet in at a very low price point (total
duplex cost is about 400k) and pretty low risk. There’s a potential I would
do both, or one of them and a 2 bed condo more for myself/to rent out as a
1 bedroom with the caveat I’d be there 2-3 months per year and store some
stuff in a bedroom

I’ll probably start making offers in 2-3 weeks once I’ve felt out the big
one, but may jump on the duplex since it would only be like 20k down and
likely would be cash positive almost immediately




On Fri, Jun 9, 2023 at 15:13 Christine Leuthold <

---
date: 2023-06-09
to: Dean Eisner <dean.eisner@gmail.com>
subject: Re: peggy noonan
msg_id: 188a0886cca74134
---

Nice thought, but the idea of the dems and gop giving up their fundraising
duopoly seems unlikely. There was the biggest push to do this in probably
25 years last cycle with Andrew Yang and a few moderates across the isle,
and it got literally nowhere. Even countries with much stricter campaign
finance reform and protection of independent parties (but where it’s
relatively easy to corrupt) like the UK are pretty rapidly sliding towards
two party dominance (really already have). And both the gop and dems have
shown zero interest in reducing corruption or making the playing field more
level (the gop has increased winner take all primaries from 7 last cycle to
20+ this)

Negative content is about 33% more engaged with than positive content on
social media. It’s much easier to coalesce people against one common enemy
than to rally them around one of multiple causes/positions

Hey man,

Thanks for this and great to chat a bit more and walk through more of the
place with you today. The modelling is super helpful to start to get a bit
of a picture.

I did the walk through this evening as well with Randall, Milo and a few
other people’s agent at OConnor (who also has a builders license), who
certainly validated that he thought it was very interesting, but also
raised a few interesting questions and takes.

I’m probably going to have a pretty hectic few days until Tuesday, but then
should have a very quiet few weeks. Let me organise my various notes and
other thoughts a bit more and let’s plan to do a call sometime Weds to Fri
next week. I’ll also have had a chance to run by the project you’re
developing with my dad by then and see if there might be a good line of
intro to the Schostaks.

Looking forward to talking more, and let me know when works for you next
week.

Have a good weekend!

Matthew

Hi Lori,

Thanks for this and your time on the phone yesterday - I will forward along
the statement momentarily.

If you could also please confirm how I might go about getting pre-approved
for a 7 unit, 2 building property purchase (4766 Commonwealth), that would
also be aprpeciated. There is a potential I would go 50/50 (or somewhere
close to that) with a friend who currently lives in the building, which I
imagine changes/potentially complicates the deal/loan somewhat. I have
heard from a broker that it would be very difficult to get a loan on this
however (although there is a chance we could look at splitting it into two
properties, permitting allowing), and in general there is a lot of
complexity to be hashed through around this, although it is becoming a
serious consideration.

I also did a viewing today of 1346 Bagley , which is definitely the most
interesting condo/townhome I’ve seen thus far. It would be good to maybe
use this as a more likely case of the transaction that might take place. I
also would likely consider buying a second unit as an investment/rental in
the same building, but would likely do that on a longer time scale (unless
there were significant financial upsides in either the purchase price or
loan as a result of batching them).

Also, I did want to mention, I could pretty easily put more than 25% down
if it would make sense from a cash flow and interest standpoint. I would
need to do a bit of modelling on my side however to make sure it made sense
for me.

Let me know if you need anything else from me!

Matthew

Plz don’t share, but curious your take on some of this.

Ignore point 1

Point 2 is a property that I was looking at that I thought was 4 units in 1
building for 1m. It’s actually 7 units in 2 for 1m, and my friend, who is
developing a large block of mixed use property a few blocks north, lives in
the building and might be interested in going in with me. Does the math
make sense? This one has huge added value to me bc he lives there (I would
maybe be there 3 months a year), friends a block away, in a good
neighborhood, has a lot of room for improvement

Point 3 and the attachements is the property he’s developing. He owns the
building and has option to purchase the rest of the block and some lots
adjacent, and is holding off financing because the market cooled and he
wants to get farther along with the initial building refurb. Any interest
in talking with him? Please do not share this, he asked me to send a more
redacted version, but that’s more for a large real estate
development/holding family I know here, and you’re you :) )

Hope you’re having fun in… Tulsa? LA? Hawaii? !!



---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: R. Philip Lockwood <rphiliplockwood@gmail.com>
Date: Wed, Jun 7, 2023 at 16:35
Subject: Detroit Real Estate Opportunities
To: <matteisn@gmail.com>


Matt,

Good catching up with you.  A few of the promised items:

*1. Brokers*
O'Connor Realty <https://www.oconnordetroit.com>
Historic Realty Detroit <https://historicrealtydetroit.com>
Loft Warehouse <https://www.theloftwarehouse.com>
Liz Tintinalli <https://lizindetroit.com/listings/>

*2. 4766 Commonwealth*
Decent property in a pretty prime location.  As I mentioned, a little rough
around the edges, but probably a growth opportunity as a rental income
property with fairly minimal investment, or flexible to convert to small
condos, too.  My rent is currently $1,100 monthly with a $35 water fee.
With brand new kitchen and baths, I bet these units would rent for around
$1,750/month.  It's managed by a 3rd party management company, The Home
Store <https://thehomestoresite.com>, but could probably be self managed to
squeeze a little extra cash out of.

"Napkin" proforma:  7 standard units x $1,100 and 1 XL unit (spiral stair)
x 1,900 = $115,200 in annual revenue.  OpEx is probably at ~45%, so Net
Operating Income of about $65,000 annually.  Valuations in real estate are
based on "capitalization rate", which for this property I'd probably run at
~7.5%.   $65,000 / 7.5% = ~$865,000 in value.  If you were to make an
offer, I'd probably start in the range of $775,000 - 825,000.  The property
likely also needs $50,000 - 75,000 in deferred maintenance, at a minimum.

Depending on how you feel about the property after tomorrow's full tour, we
can discuss a potential joint investment in further detail, but I could
probably pull together 50% of the acquisition price in equity.

*3. XYZ Development Woodbridge Projects*
-Danish Brotherhood Hall: basic overview document attached--hasn't been
updated in a few months so there may be some discrepancies, but it captures
the project fairly well.
-"12th & Forest" (new construction project on vacant land)":  Design Review
deck, attached.  This is the latest .PDF I used for the City's site plan
approval process, so it's more design focused than development, but
showcases the project accurately enough.

Let me know if you have any questions or want input as the real estate
search continues.

Best,
-Philip

 12F_Design Review_23-0525rpl.pdf
<https://drive.google.com/file/d/1r0fZIirR0wRu9--f2wsQWO0zvgZkYoxg/view?usp=drive_web>

Hi Randall,

Sorry for the double (triple?) emails, but wanted to give you a brief
update.

I met with a friend who happens to live in 4766 Commonwealth and is a real
estate developer redeveloping the Danish community center and surrounding
area a few blocks north. He clarified to me that the listing is in fact 7
units in 2 building, and gave me a quick walkthrough of his apartment and
some background context.

From an economic standpoint this is extremely interesting given there are 3
more units than I realised (I have some basic back of napkin math he sent
me, which suggests hopefully we could also get buy price down into high
800s or low 900s). It also has a huge convenience factor with him living
there (and it sounds like already coordinating a fair amount of
maintenance). Moreover, it sounds like he might be interesting in going in
up to 50% on the deal with me (and Nick had expressed interest as well).

All to say would love to do a full walk through, and that seems like an
even more viable option than I realised. I also saw there was an open house
there tomorrow afternoon, and heard the selling agent wanted to be there
for all viewings, so if there is a better time to go, please do let me
know. We could also probably meet my friend who lives there if it was
before 5.30, although it sounds like he’s heading up north at 6. But if
it’s after 6, it sounds like Nick can join. So whatever makes the most
sense from your perspective there!

Looking forward to meeting tomorrow.

Best,

Matthew

Hi Jerome,

Thanks for these. In terms of timing, would tomorrow after 3.30pm,
Wednesday after 3.30pm work for you? I could also do Wednesday before noon,
and am wide open on Friday.

Let me know what works best for you. It's easy enough to meet you at one of
the Corktown locations and go from there if that makes the most sense - I'm
staying with a friend who is living in the apartment building around UFO
Factory at Michigan and Trumbull.


I've also included a few more detailed notes below around some of the
properties you mentioned and other points, as well as changed 2 or 3 of the
fit labels in the spreadsheet based on your feedback.

The Gratiot building is definitely interesting, although a little concerned
about first floor on street (unless maybe it could be zoned
commercial/mixed-use) and lack of outdoor space (is there roof access, an
alley?). But definitely something I would consider/want to see.

On the E Grand unit, it is a little farther out than I'd like to be, but
maybe doable. I really like the other unit in this building with the roof
access, but being a 1 bedroom makes it kind of tricky from an economic
perspective (although if I maybe went the route of buying two separate
units over the course of a year or so, that could make more sense as the
unit to use for myself - but I think it was also much more expensive).

In general "good economic sense" probably will drive about 80% of my
decision making process. 2020 Junction is also in line with this thinking,
and particularly as an investment/rental unit that I likely would not live
in myself, maybe in combination with the Brooklyn unit for myself/a
flatmate if the price was slashed (although I imagine rental income might
not cover both mortgages).

I also like the idea of the 11th Street units as a way to buy multiple
units in the same building/close to each other. I feel like STR is probably
a bit of a double edged sword though?

All the rest sounds interesting as well. On taxes, I imagine those are
annual recurring? I'm a little wary of the idea of a high recurring cost.


Let me know if there's anything else!

Best,

Matthew

date: 2023-06-05
to: Colin Allison <colin@konnectventures.co>
subject: Macro questions I had
msg_id: 1888be6480976589
---

Is the vision for this that this is one of a few/multiple
partnerships/funds that we would spin out? Or are we basically getting deep
into bed with Moonpay? If so, may be good to talk to my friend Adrian who
is their head of strategy just to get another take on how he would see the
deal in the scope of what Moonpay is doing.

Is the $500k pre-seed investment in each venture based on any requirements
or litmus/validation test? Does Moonpay have veto rights or do we have full
decision making control? What are governance mechanisms/decision points?

Generally a little hazy on ownership structure. Does Moonpay own 80% of
Konnect, and Konnect owns all the ventures? How is it determined how much
of a venture’s equity will be sold in a funding round? I see some of this
in financials spreadsheet

Worth talking to a lawyer (my friend Evan potentially?) if Cayman is the
place that makes the most sense to incorporate?

---
date: 2023-06-05
to: Jerome Dixon <jerome@residentialrome.com>
subject: Re: Opportunities in Detroit for Matt
msg_id: 18889da33050d93c
---

Sorry for the double emails - a friend of mine also mentioned some soon to
be completed/in progress units by RCH on/around Horton between Brush and
John R I'd also potentially be interested in seeing, and maybe also bumps
the E Grand Blvd property up my list a little bit as it's validating of the
area/I realize I have some friends that own property around there.

But in general I'd say I'm maybe leaning towards something a bit more
expensive and multi-family at this point over a condo/single family unit if
possible given having a more budget than I realized.

---
date: 2023-06-05
to: Jerome Dixon <jerome@residentialrome.com>
subject: Re: Opportunities in Detroit for Matt
msg_id: 18889c906600e3b1
---

Hi Jerome,

All sounds great. I've gone through all the listings you sent and a few
others (in this sheet
<https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1M1BNgCblTGcWSl3eyrrKXxNXNTF_NayYVSjlZODPVDk/edit#gid=155769907>
on
the "Listings" tabs. I've also added quite a bit more detail about my
criteria on the "Criteria" tab). The ones I would be especially interested
in viewing are:

   - 2003 Brooklyn Street 405 (very close to where I'm currently staying)
   - 438 Selden Street 203
   - 7874 Van Dyke Pl
   - 1228 25th St
   - 4154 2nd Ave 7
   - 2037 11th St (very close to where I'm currently staying)
   - 2643 Brush Street 17
   - 2020 Junction Ave
   - 4766 Commonwealth


I'd also be interested in viewing the following:

   - 3408 Cochrane Street 1
   - 1350 Bagley Street (very close to where I'm currently staying)
   - 2843 E Grand Blvd 4
   - 291 Edmund 2 (very close to where I'm currently staying - I actually
   walked by here today and could definitely be interested depending on price)
   - 2133 Field St


I have also confirmed, I can extend my budget a quite a bit if necessary,
especially for something multi-unit like the 4766 Commonwealth quad. I
would prefer not to if possible, but this also maybe opens up an
opportunity to buy a unit for myself and a unit for rental elsewhere
(potentially the 2020 Junction Ave unit, as it seems extremely cheap,
although I'd worry a bit something might be wrong with it it's so cheap). I
do think I am starting to land a bit more that I would prefer to be on the
West Side (although do not want to exclude looking at the East Side), and
would strongly prefer to purchase multiple units (whether in the same
building or two separate properties).

Also, a couple of general points I’d be curious your insight on (although
easy enough to discuss when we meet in person:

   - How difficult is it to remove tenants here (I.e. if I wanted to take
   one for myself)
   - How strict is zoning? I had envisioned doing this a few years down the
   line when I had more capital to play with, but have always been interested
   in the idea of putting together a mixed use
   residential/coworking/events-type hybrid space
   - What is the likelihood of not qualifying for the NEZ tax abatement, or
   is it a relatively straightforward/predictable process?
   - Also just to note, I can extend my budget a bit, although would
   ideally find something below $5-600k. That being said, I have confirmed I
   could theoretically round up enough funds for the down payment on something
   closer to 1m.


Let me know your thoughts/next steps.

Best,

Matthew

difficult on the west side.

Also my week is filling in a bit, but I’m still free Tuesday from 2-5 or
Thursday after 2.30 or anytime Friday afternoon if any of those times work
for you/we want to pencil something in.

Best,

Matthew

Hi Randall,

I'm in Detroit looking at a real estate purchase over the coming 10 days or
so, and my roommate from college/good friend Nick Inchaustegui recommended
I get in touch with you.

I'm looking to buy a 2-3 bedroom house, duplex, or maybe apartment
(potentially as few as one bedroom, or as many as 5 or 6 if duplex),
ideally closing in September, with a price range of $400k-$750k in the
following areas: Woodbridge, Pallister Park, Virginia Park, Boston Edison,
Midtown, Corktown, New Center, North End, Southwest/Mexican Town, Brush
Park, Lasalle Gardens.

I'm trying to do as many viewings as I can to get a feel for various areas
and get the ball rolling. Let me know if it might make sense to jump on a
call tomorrow or meet next week, or if you have any listings I could look
at.

Best,

Matthew

money from Grammy, I can probably foot up to $150k, although would prefer
to do a little less.


Looking to buy a 2-3 bedroom house, duplex, or maybe apartment (potentially
as few as one bedroom, or as many as 5 or 6 if duplex), ideally closing in
September, with a price range of $400k-$750k in the following areas:
Woodbridge, Pallister Park, Virginia Park, Boston Edison, Midtown,
Corktown, New Center, North End, Southwest/Mexican Town, Brush Park,
Lasalle Gardens. I'm planning to be around til June 10th to do viewings,
but could extend that another week or so relatively easily. Looking for any
recommendations, opportunities, insights, arbitrage, etc.

date: 2023-05-24

subject: Re: In Michigan early June

It's a little convoluted - the simple is I'd love to see you Friday as
well, but don't want to make you drive into town/campus if it's
inconvenient for what might be a pretty quick visit. But let's definitely
do Monday - I just sent along a calendar invite, and can meet you at your
office if that's easiest for you (or wherever).

The long story for Friday is two of my good friends from college are
getting married next spring, so she's going to try on a wedding dress in
Ann Arbor on Friday, and the groom-to-be and I are going to run a few
errands around town in the meantime, and then it sounds like we're going to
go to Zingerman's for a late lunch and Dom's for a drink after (although it
seems like quite a bit of our plans are up in the air). If you were in the
office I could probably drop by or meet somewhere downtown (or at Dom's)
for a coffee/drink, but it might be a bit quick/susceptible to last minute
rescheduling if the day gets away from us a bit.

Let me know what you think re: Friday, and really looking forward to
getting to catch up :)

date: 2023-05-23
to: f@swipelux.com
subject: Following up

Hey Filip,

Hope all is well!  I just wanted to follow up from our call a few weeks ago
(sorry for the delay, very hectic period of time).

Do want to schedule a call for next week to do a deep dive into some topics
around analytics, mar/sales tech, growth/product, etc we had touched on?
I'll be in Eastern US time, so it will be easier to align our times than
usual. Thursday/Friday I'm especially free.

Let me know when might work for you!

Best,

Matthew

Hi Julie,

As I mentioned on our call, please meet Colin, who is an absolute
operations powerhouse and has a very interesting background across
accelerators, management/innovation consulting, and early stage venture.
He’s currently leading our efforts to set up a web3 venture studio and
fund, and is who I generally go to when I need to get organised or
strategise about tech/business.

Colin, please meet Julie, who Qomoo connected me with, and is the COO at
Blockworks. She was interested in connecting with people in interesting
operations and cross-functional roles, and a lot of the questions she was
asking when we connected seemed very much up your alley.

I’ll let you two take it from here!

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2023-05-17
to: Patrick Sheehan <psheehan@elevationsecurities.com>
subject: Re: Catch up + Monzo
msg_id: 1882884e9883ff68
---

Sounds good.

Just to clarify, are you only interested in buying from Tom? My friend
suggested his best connection to Tom would likely be via his friend the
ex-CFO, who also might be interested in selling some shares.

My friend is getting out of knee surgery, so a bit less responsive than
normal, but let me know if you might be interested in talking to the ex-CFO
first/as a way in.

Out of curiosity, what’s the interest in Monzo vs Revolut? I know their
manège ment team and funding is a bit sketchy and they’re a bit more
controversial, but their economics, product, and traction generally seem
much better (although maybe farther away from exit liquidity?). Always
happy to talk challenger banks, I’ve used quite a few of them at this point
:)

Would love to connect with Will - I’ll probably be out there again in late
June/early July, but feel free to intro in the mean time as well if useful.


On Fri, May 12, 2023 at 12:20 Patrick Sheehan <
psheehan@elevationsecurities.com> wrote:

date: 2023-05-12
to: Patrick Sheehan <psheehan@elevationsecurities.com>
subject: Re: Catch up + Monzo
msg_id: 18810acacc7a97e5
---

Hi Pat,

Great to hear from you. I’ve pinged a friend of mine who is super connected
in EU startups and fintech who if he doesn’t have a direct contact, almost
certainly has a second degree connection.

Maybe a little bit left field given their market focus, but I do know the
founders here, which is probably one of the top 3 or so challenger bank
startups in Mexico if you’d potentially want to connect:
https://miflink.com/

Let me know if there’s anything else I might be able to help with :)

Hope all is well,

Matthew



On Thu, May 11, 2023 at 13:33 Patrick Sheehan <
psheehan@elevationsecurities.com> wrote:

---
date: 2023-05-12
to: "Paweł Tomczuk" <pawel.m.tomczuk@gmail.com>
subject: Fwd: Catch up + Monzo
msg_id: 18810a8d5afb462e
---

Total shot in the dark, but do you know or have a solid connection to Tom
Blomfield? One of my good friends for 15+ years’s dad runs a very
interesting finance advisory/services org (honestly a lot of what they do
is a bit over my head) that often facilitates secondary sales of shares
from employees of mid-to-late stage companies (I know they have a pretty
significant stake in Epic Games and have helped quite a few of their
employees sell some shares early), and is keen to try and connect.



---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Patrick Sheehan <psheehan@elevationsecurities.com>
Date: Thu, May 11, 2023 at 13:33
Subject: Catch up + Monzo
To: Matthew Eisner <matteisn@gmail.com>
CC: John Sheehan <jsheehan@elevationsecurities.com>, Smoot Carter <
scarter@elevationsecurities.com>


Hi Matt

I hope that all is well. I understand from Emma that you might know or have
connection with Tom Blomfield, founder and ex CEO of Monzo. We have a
corporate client that we know well and one that generally has a good
reputation that is interested in buying Monzo shares. We would like to
reach out to Tom and discuss Monzo and any desire that he might have to
sell some of his shares.

I have attached John and Smoot who would lead the outreach.

Thanks for entertaining an introduction and we hope to see you soon!

All the best,

Pat

[image: image001.png]

Patrick Sheehan
CEO

6836 Carnegie Blvd
<https://www.google.com/maps/search/6836+Carnegie+Blvd?entry=gmail&source=g>
.
*W:* +1 (704) 926-1106

Suite 240
*M:* +1 (704) 340-5892

Charlotte, NC 28211 USA
www.elevationsecurities.com
*psheehan@elevationsecurities.com <psheehan@elevationsecurities.com>*

date: 2023-05-12
to: Andry Lebedev <a@swipelux.com>
subject: Re: Follow up - Swipelux
msg_id: 1880e01e0996a296
---

Hi Andry,

Thanks for your email and sorry for the late reply - I’ve been having quite
a hectic week of travel.

I do want to move forward, but checking some of my budgets and upcoming
investments I’m looking to make (assuming none fall through), it’d be a lot
easier for me to make a $5k investment, even at the higher valuation if the
lower is not possible.

Happy to jump on a call next week if that’d help to discuss. I also owe
Filip and email to do a bit of a deeper dive on where I might be able to
help with things.

Hope all is well,

Matthew

and I would generally expect us to be keeping Xero up to date (or at least
not have a nearly 6 month lag). I had hoped what you sent me before was
sufficient despite not being all of what I asked for or the same
documentation provided in other years, but it turns out it is not.

Please let me know when it can be completed. End of next month is okay for
me, any later than that is not.

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2023-05-03
to: Sam Cobbina <Sam@shieldaccountancy.co.uk>
subject: Re: Annual Statement/P & L for Vang Advisory Ltd
msg_id: 187e34ab92cc4ae8
---

Hi Sam,

Thanks for this. My US accountant also needed a balance sheet. I attempted
to generate this in Xero (see attached), but my US accountant has pointed
out some issues - see below:

"On Vang Advisory Limited's 2022 balance sheet:

   - The fixed assets that were disposed of in the 2021 financial
   statements have returned for 2021 and 2022.
   - The 4,343 provision for corporation tax in 2021 and 2022 is actually
   the balance from 2020 (two years ago). The balance in the 2021 financial
   statements was 6,964.
   - The current year earnings 4,977 disagrees with the 8,785 shown on the
   profit & loss



Can you ask your accounting folks to update this and send a corrected 2022
financial statements?"



Is there a way we can update/fix these, or can you send me a corrected
version? There is not a huge amount of urgency around this as I filed an
extension this year, but would be good to have by the end of May or so.


Thanks,


Matthew

---
date: 2023-04-28
to: Brian Leidel <bleidel@kbgrp.com>
subject: Re: Questions
msg_id: 187c950973df946a
---

Hi Brian,

Thanks for this.

I will see if I can generate the balance sheet myself in Xero so we don’t
have to wait for my UK accountants. My July 2021-June 2022 tax return
should be in the file I sent (as well as in last years). The filing for
July 2022-June 2023 is not due in the UK until August or September, so I
will not have it before then.

Makes sense re medical. Is there a limit to how much can be run through VA
in terms of meals, accommodation, etc?

Sounds good re: residency. You mentioned a guide/attachment but I don’t
believe it made it in to this email, if you could send it again.

I will start labelling the receipts from now on. However, especially if I
am expensing meals, I am very likely to be well beyond 50 transactions,
even for 2023.

Thanks,

Matthew

---
date: 2023-04-27
to: f@swipelux.com
subject: Re: Invitation: Matthew Eisner and Filip Kollert @ Thu 2023-04-27 12:00 - 12:30 (EDT) (matteisn@gmail.com)

Hey Filip,

Sorry for the late notice, but I’m running a little behind getting back
from the doctors office. I should be on the call about 5-10 minutes late.

Talk soon!

Matthew

date: 2023-04-25
to: Brian Leidel <bleidel@kbgrp.com>
subject: Re: Questions
msg_id: 187b92f5f8a28c34
---

Hi Brian,

Thanks for this.

Regarding the Vang Advisory 2022 statements, is the attached financial
statement sufficient, or do you need additional information?

For expenses last year, my medical expenses were only about $7k. I had not
been running them through Vang Advisory. I only just received my business
debit card, so will start doing so for travel, equipment, meals (is there a
limit to this?) from now on.

In terms of residency, so I understand, it does not matter how much time I
spend in Florida, as long as I spend less than 183 days in California or
any other individual state? Or do I need to spend more time in Florida than
California?

In terms for transaction volume, I may come under 50 transactions this
year, but most years I would likely be over that threshold. I can certainly
file all receipts as I have been in a Google Drive folder. I had not been
labelling the receipts, let me know if I should do so going forward.

For IRA contributions, I am currently using Robinhood and Acorns to manage
these (I put in about $1.5k to each last year, and am planning to do $6k
per year going forward). I do not believe they have sent out the 5498 (as I
was making contributions right up until the deadline), but will forward to
you when I receive it.

And let me know if there's anything else you need from me. Thanks!

Matthew

---
date: 2023-04-24
to: Andry Lebedev <a@swipelux.com>
subject: Re: Follow up - Swipelux
msg_id: 187b37a7ce8308a7
---

Hi Andry,
If you can get me in at the previous valuation for $10k, that would be great.
$5k at 12 could also work. Let's do another call around roadmap (scheduled for
Weds), and then I also wanted to do another call to get an overview of some
data/analytics/growth stack you're working with (booked for Thurs) and then
hopefully we have everything around investment sorted by then (but also no rush
on my side).
Re: MoonPay, the VC friend I've spoken to hasn't mentioned you to them, but he
knows their head of investments, who he thought it would be interesting to. I
also know the head of strategy there, but I haven't reached out to him about
this. But sounds good, will mention to the VC friend not to mention to them.
Let me know if there's anything else!
Best,
Matthew

date: 2023-04-20
to: Andry Lebedev <a@swipelux.com>
subject: Re: Follow up - Swipelux
msg_id: 187a0d7ed2833718
---

Oh, I forgot to add, i passed  your deck to a friend who runs a venture
studio/fund in Singapore, and he has a contact at Moonpay that might be
interested to invest/support - do you want an intro or do you see them as too
competitive? Wanted to check first

---
date: 2023-04-20
to: Andry Lebedev <a@swipelux.com>
subject: Re: Follow up - Swipelux
msg_id: 187a0bf7e9512a5d
---

Hi Andry,

I had a good conversation with Filip, and introed him to a contact of mine
who is quite looped in to the Algorand ecosystem and grant programs, as
well as helps startups raise funding and secure high value partnerships -
hopefully that proves to be fruitful. There are a few other intros that
might be useful I'll look to make as well.

I've also had a chance to look through all your docs. They generally look
good to me. I know I'm already stretching your min ticket a bit, but I did
want to ask if there was any we could go down to $5k? If not, $10k would
probably work as well.

Again, happy to do this as solely an equity investment, or with some sort
of combined investment with discounted advisory arrangement (which could be
useful if we want to try and raise the value of my investment, as I know
I'm a small ticket) - I'm quite flexible.

Let me know what makes sense to you.

Best,

Matthew

On Thu, 20 Apr 2023 at 11:27, Joe (Pave) <support@portify.zendesk.com>
wrote:

---
date: 2023-04-20
to: team@paveapp.com
subject: Cancelling account
msg_id: 1879f3b0ecc197e8
---

I want to cancel my account and return my funds. I believe I have had it
for more than 12 months, and seen no impact, let alone information related
to my credit score, but I cannot see the full payment history or how much I
have deposited. I also cannot see any way to return these funds to my
possession. These are significant transparency issues when considering a
consumer finance app. Please advise

---
date: 2023-04-20
to: Ilir Orana <ilir@clearaccesslaw.com>
subject: Re: Legal Opinion Letter: Required Documents
msg_id: 1879f379ad0c63d4
---

Hi Ilir,

Good to hear. The charge in Georgia was resolved via something called a
pre-trial diversion program and (I believe) subsequently dismissed or
expunged. I believe it is particularly designed for youth offenders of
misdemeanours, but I’m not even sure if the program still exists. It was a
long time ago so I don’t really remember, but let me know if you need me to
request any records around this.

Thanks,

Matthew

---
date: 2023-04-20
to: Brian Leidel <bleidel@kbgrp.com>
subject: Questions
msg_id: 1879eeb8e6202e4b
---

Hi Brian,

Hope all is well with you. I had a couple of queries I wanted to run by you
and make sure I’m planning properly.

First, let me know if there is any additional info you need regarding my
filing for 2022 (I saw the notification come through that an extension had
been filed).

Second, is it worthwhile for me to collect the receipts/expenses around my
medical costs related to my shoulder injury last spring for deductions?
Anything else that I should be looking to deduct (housing (where I often
work), travel, work supplies, etc)?

Third, I switched my employment from California to Florida at the end of
last year. Are there any travel/time in state requirements to be considered
a Florida resident for 2023 I need to ensure to meet? My drivers license,
bank accounts, and permanent address have been here for 10+ years, but I
was getting paid in and had a mailing address in California last year. I
had heard 183 days, more days than any other US state, and a few others.

Fourth, I’ve started to have some transactions on my US Vang Advisory
Limited account. In the past, I’ve simply collected receipts in a folder to
share. Should I do a similar thing for you? Or is there any way in
particular you would prefer me to track income/expenses? Do we need to
connect my bank account to Xero?

Fifth, I’ve made a few IRA contributions this year, and am planning to try
and max it out going forward. Is there anything I need to include around
this on my tax filings, or is this done when withdrawing from the IRA?

Let me know if any of these are better answered by someone else, and thanks
for all your help.

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2023-04-19
to: f@swipelux.com, qomooatomsa@gmail.com
subject: Filip/Qomoo intro
msg_id: 18797479a17b742e
---

Hi Filip,

Please meet Qomoo, who I mentioned is quite close with the grants,
investment, and partnership arms of a few blockchains, including Algorand.
He also does some solid work helping startups raise and secure
partnerships, so might be good to chat with him on that front too.

Qomoo, please meet Filip, who is the COO at Swipelux, which I mentioned to
you earlier. I’m potentially looking to invest/advise them, but in parallel
to that, Filip was asking me when I spoke to him earlier today if I knew a
better point of contact than who they currently have at Algorand, and you
came to mind.

I’ll let you two take it from here!

Best,

Matthew

Best,

Matthew

date: 2023-04-12
to: qomooatomsa@gmail.com
subject: partesia proposal
msg_id: 18772dbc3e00685d
---

document outline here:
https://docs.google.com/document/d/1yGybQ4E0j1WZvPYiG9MOCZX_DjcugtNyEwfxAAD_tz4/edit

these were my ideas for what we could offer:

Core offerings (we should try and move 2 of these to additional services):

   - Hackathon to engage corporate partners
   - Project/startup mentor matchmaking event series
   - Corporate/late stage partnership program
   - Partnership/late-stage accelerator
   - Multi-stage grant programs

Additional services:

   - Syndicating deals
   - Tokenization of deals


Any to you jump out as more or less relevant. Also worth noting that
typically it's easier to get traction on these sort of projects starting
with earlier stage projects and building to later over time, but I'm open
to trying to start with later stage ones

date: 2023-04-04
to: Ben Clarke <ben@clearaccesslaw.com>
subject: Re: Entering Canada with Old OWI
msg_id: 1874db397266b029
---

Hi Ben,

Very much appreciate the detailed information here. Generally the rate
sounds reasonable, but it would be good to know what the success rate is of
this (compared to Temporary Resident Permit or Criminal Rehabilitation,
which sound like they would not be

I've attached the records I could find via the Michigan and Georgia court
online databases. Let me know if this is adequate, or I would need more
(the Georgia one seems a bit thin/vague).

It appears my OWI charge probation ended on 4 April 2012, and the initial
charge occured on 13 Nov 2010 and sentencing on 28 April 2011.

To get a better understanding, is there a potential that the Canadian
immigration would reject a Legal Opinion Letter at the border? Or do they
need to approve it ahead of time?

Also, I was hoping to be able to enter Canada in early July. Is it possible
to complete this process by then?

Generally the rate sounds reasonable, but it would be good to know what the
success rate is of this (and compared to Temporary Resident Permit or
Criminal Rehabilitation, but which sound like they would not be ready in
time for the trip I was hoping to take).

Thanks,

Matthew

date: 2023-03-30
to: MatthewBowenEsq@gmail.com, helen@distributedfinance.co
subject: Helen/Matthew intro
msg_id: 18733dfe4aabee76
---

Hi Matthew,

Please meet Helen, who is the GC at zest, which I mentioned has been
working on a number of projects around web3-based investment, but most
relevantly to you, managing the set up of investment DAOs including
Borderless's. They were in the accelerator program I managed for Algorand,
and one of the few companies I made an investment in from the cohort, so
needless to say I quite like what they're building.

Helen, please meet Matthew, who has a very interesting background in
finance and securities law in the US. He's been working on some very
interesting projects around tokenization of securities, as well as a number
of on-chain communities and tooling for governance, and looking for people
to work with around that, as well as to expand to additional blockchains.

I'll let you two take it from here!

Matthew

---
date: 2023-03-30
to: pawel@techtree.dev, evanzin@gmail.com
subject: Pawel/Evan Intro
msg_id: 18733da4e6c9d5ac
---

Hi Evan,

Please meet Pawel, who I think I mentioned to you while we were at
ETHDenver. He has a long background in entrepreneurship, most recently as
the founder of TechTree (which I think you probably remember I used to work
at and am an investor in). His first business was a professional services
business that he eventually sold to Publicis, and he used to do quite a bit
of advisory for professional services businesses (and was hugely helpful
back when I was setting up and running my consulting business).

Pawel, please meet Evan, a friend of mine from college I mentioned on our
call earlier today. He's been the crypto lead at Reed Smith for a number of
years, and is now planning to launch his own legal practice/consultancy. He
probably has some thoughts re: launching tokens (although it sounds like
your thinking is very similar on the current risk around this in the US),
and would probably benefit from some of your expertise with advisory and
service businesses.

I'll let you two take it from here!

Matthew

date: 2023-03-29
to: pawel@techtree.dev
subject: Dev shop or senior technical hire
msg_id: 1872ed868cbda3d2
---

Hey Pawel,

Hope all is well with you!

My technical partner at my project on Samba is having to step back into an
advisory role due to some family matters, and I need to find a replacement
pretty quickly. We're discussing a few alternatives and I thought either
you/your network or TechTree might be able to help.

We're potentially looking at bringing on a dev shop in Poland, as we've
been setting up a fair amount of our engineering there and in Taiwan. I
think we have one in mind, but if you have any recommendations of a solid
one with experience on mobile apps, adtech, customer data, blockchain, etc,
I'd love to talk to them.

Also in the more mid-term, we're looking to find someone who can replace
Brandon <https://www.linkedin.com/in/brandonooi/> as an Engineering Lead or
CTO/co-founder. I should be getting a JD later today, and we should have a
relatively normal budget for a referral fee on this. Happy to run this
through TechTree, but also if you know anyone personally who might make
sense for this, it'd be great to connect.

Let me know if there's anything I can help on re: TechTree, and would be
great to catch up soon! I'll also likely be in Europe for a month or two in
June/July, so would be great to catch up then.

Best,

Matthew

---
date: 2023-03-29
to: Jeffrey QUINT <jeff@sledgehammerpd.com>
subject: Re: SEO analysis
msg_id: 1872eb4f6c438fae
---

HI Jeffrey,

Thanks for reaching out, and glad the article was useful :)

It's been a year or so since I've done hands on SEO so am a bit out of
touch with the cutting edge best practices (although generally the
underlying principles and logic usually remain the same). Local SEO (which
if the resounding majority of your business comes from the LA area, is what
you'd need), was also never my specialty.

But more than happy to jump on a call for 30 minutes and hear a bit about
the stage you're at and what your SEO provider has been doing to
sense-check if it seems like a quality approach at a reasonable price, and
maybe make some recommendations for actions/alternative providers/etc. Just
let me know some times next week that might work for you.

You might also check growthmentor.com. I used to mentor with them when I
was more active in marketing, and there are some very talented people there
who give their time away for free (and can be a good place to find an
agency/consultant).

Best,

Matthew
